Both the dial and case are in great cosmetic, untouched condition. Ready to wear!

title: Bulgari Diagno-White Dial
description: Here's a vintage Bvlgari reference LCV35 automatic with a white dial from the 1990s. Keeps great time and is fully functional. Measurements include a 35mm steel case and 20mm lug width. It's on its original bracelet that fits a 7-inch wrist. Quickset date function at 3 o'clock. Steel case and caseback. All parts are original, including the signed Bvlgari crown, dial, movement, and caseback. Both the dial and case are in great cosmetic, untouched condition. Ready to wear!

title: Le coultre - Black Arabic Numeral
description: Here's a vintage LeCoultre in 14K yellow gold manual wind with a black Arabic numeral dial from the 1960s. On an aftermarket black leather strap. “VXN’ initials at 6 o'clock. Measurements include a 34mm case and 18mm lug width. All parts, including the crown, dial, movement, and caseback are original. Ready to wear!

title: 1969 Rolex Cellini 3805 - Mocha Dial
description: A Cellini with warmth and age This Rolex Cellini comes from the late 1960s into the early 1970s, when the Cellini line leaned into elegant shapes and softer design language rather than tool-watch toughness. The tonneau-style case gives the watch gentle presence on the wrist, while the mocha sunburst dial adds warmth that feels unmistakably period-correct. It’s a dress Rolex that doesn’t try to be sharp or modern — it’s comfortable in its own era. Why this configuration works The sunburst champagne dial has deepened over time, developing spotted patina around the edges that adds texture and tells the story of the watch likely coming from a saltier reigon of the world. It gives the watch character and reinforces its vintage identity instead of flattening it into something overly restored. The yellow gold case frames the dial naturally, and the overall size keeps the watch elegant and wearable for a wide range of wrists. This is Cellini design doing what it was meant to do: stay refined without relying on complication or scale. Condition is key Condition is honest and consistent with age. The case presents well with light wear, the dial shows visible spotting and edge degradation that should be expected and appreciated on a watch of this era, and the crystal presents cleanly. The watch is fitted with an aftermarket leather strap and gold buckle, both are new. When we got the watch in we wanted to make sure the patina was only presnt on the dial, in which it was. The manual-wind movement is fully functional, running at in great shape after passing by our test bench A vintage Rolex that wears easily This isn’t a pristine or untouched Cellini — it’s a watch with real age and personality. The patinated dial, gold case, and classic shape make it an easy choice for someone who values character over perfection. Dress it up or down, this can be your daily, or one for special occasions.

title: 1987 Rolex OP 15037 - Yellow Gold Mocha Dial
description: A Warm Palette In 14K Gold The 15037 is a transitional reference of the 34mm Oyster Perpetual Date executed in 14k yellow gold. This alloy provides a slightly softer and paler hue compared to 18k, giving the piece a distinct mid-century character. The defining feature is the mocha dial, a rich and earthy tone that creates a monochromatic aesthetic when paired with the gold fluted bezel and Italian leather strap. It is a configuration that moves away from the high-contrast look of standard gold Rolexes in favor of something more tonal. The Significance Of The Mocha Configuration While the 34mm Date is a staple of the catalog, the mocha dial provides a completely different wrist presence. It bridges the gap between a tool watch and a piece of jewelry, leaning into the warmth of the materials. It represents a period where Rolex was experimenting with dial tones that complemented their precious metal cases in a more integrated way. Honest Condition And History The watch is in fair condition and reflects decades of use. The 14k gold case shows visible surface wear and rounding consistent with its age. The original caseback features an engraving from a previous owner, documenting a part of the history of the watch. The mocha dial remains in good condition and is well-preserved. After passing through our test bench, the movement is running strong and is ready for regular use. A Sophisticated Tonal Alternative This is for the collector who values a specific look over a perfect case. The 34mm proportions make it an easy daily wearer that offers the prestige of a gold Rolex without the loud profile of a jubilee. The mocha dial gives the watch a depth and character that feels more intentional than a standard silver or champagne face. Dress it up or down, this could be dailied or used for your special occasions.

title: 1999 Rolex Oyster Perpetual 15200 - Black Dial
description: A Core Modern Vintage Rolex The 15200 is often the best entry point into neo-vintage Rolex because it offers modern reliability with classic proportions. While many focus on the larger Datejust, this Oyster Perpetual Date wears with a leaner and more focused profile. The combination of a smooth bezel and a black dial creates a monochromatic look that is arguably the most versatile in the catalog. It is a watch that ignores trends and focuses strictly on functional longevity. The Versatility Of Black And Steel A black dial on a stainless steel Date is a significant step up from the more common silver or champagne configurations. It anchors the watch and gives it a sporty edge that allows it to transition easily between casual and formal settings. This late nineties example benefits from a refined movement architecture, providing a mechanical backbone known for its high utility. It is a configuration that prioritizes a clean aesthetic and long term durability. Preserved Physical Condition The watch is in very good condition and shows the signs of careful ownership. The case and Oyster bracelet are clean with only the minor surface wear expected from a piece of this age. The crystal has a few very small surface scratches that do not interfere with legibility. After passing through our test bench, the automatic movement is running strong and maintaining proper time for daily use. The Final Word In Daily Wear This is the watch for the person who wants one Rolex that can handle every situation without feeling like an overstatement. It carries enough weight to feel substantial on the wrist while maintaining a footprint that tucks neatly under a cuff. It remains the standard for an everyday timepiece because it does not try to be anything more than a durable, accurate, and classically designed tool.

title: 1974 Rolex Precision Oyster 6426 - Blue Dial
description: A Pure Manual Expression The 6426 represents the most stripped-back version of the Rolex Oyster. By removing the date window and the automatic winding rotor, Rolex achieved a thinner case profile that sits significantly flatter on the wrist than its Perpetual counterparts. It is a reference that values simplicity and tactile engagement, requiring a daily wind that connects the owner to the mechanical heart of the watch. While silver and champagne are common, this blue sunburst execution provides a much higher level of visual depth. The Character Of Organic Aging The blue sunburst dial on this 1974 example has moved beyond its factory state. Over the last five decades, the original tritium lume has interacted with the dial surface, causing a specific degradation around the minute track. This has resulted in a textured, organic patina that creates a ring of character around the perimeter. It is a transition that only happens with time and environmental exposure, turning a standard production dial into a unique historical artifact that cannot be replicated. Honest Mid-Seventies Condition The watch is in good condition overall and shows the honest wear of a vintage piece from the mid-seventies. The stainless steel Oyster case retains its proper proportions with expected surface marks from regular use. The blue sunburst dial is well-preserved in the center, with the aforementioned patina concentrated around the edges. The Oyster bracelet is a correct A-stamp version with fair stretch, remaining fully functional and secure. After passing our test bench, the movement is running strong and maintaining proper time. An Entry Into High Character Vintage This is the right choice for the collector who prefers a watch with a visible history. The 34mm case size remains a benchmark for classic proportions, offering a discreet but confident presence. Because of the manual movement and the unique dial aging, it feels less like a commodity and more like a specific find. It is a versatile, steel Rolex that carries enough character to stand out in a room of standard modern watches.

title: Rodania Chronograph - Blue Dial
description: Here's a Rodania automatic chronograph with a blue sunburst dial from the 1970s. Keeps great time and is fully functional. Measurements include a 38mm case size and 20mm lug width. Non-quick set Date function at 3 o'clock. On an aftermarket steel integrated bracelet that complements the piece quite well. Lume in both hands and lume plots are still intact. White hands. White sub dial at 6 o'clock. Orange hand at 12 o'clock. Steel case and caseback. All parts are original, including the signed Omega crown, caseback, dial, and movement. Both the dial and case are in great cosmetic, untouched condition. Ready to wear!

title: Tiffany & Co x Pulsar P3 - 14K Yellow Gold
description: Retro Tech with Luxury Touches The Pulsar P3 is the definitive artifact of the 1970s digital revolution. When it launched, it wasn't viewed as a gadget, but as a high-tier luxury item that commanded prices higher than a Rolex Submariner. This specific example is housed in a substantial 14k yellow gold case, a far cry from the steel or gold-filled versions typically found. Most importantly, it features the prestigious Tiffany & Co. retailer stamp on the crystal. For the era, a Tiffany-signed Pulsar was the ultimate intersection of legacy New York luxury and the bleeding edge of American technology. A Masterclass In Space Age Design The P3 "Time Computer" represents a moment when the industry completely re-imagined how a watch should look and function. The 41x46mm cushion case is bold and architectural, designed specifically to frame the deep red LED display. This isn't a watch that is "always on"; it requires the wearer to engage with it, pushing a button to illuminate the time in glowing red digits. It is a tactile experience that harks back to the dawn of the quartz era, and in solid 14k gold, it carries a physical heft that confirms its status as a serious piece of jewelry. Solid Gold And Magnetically Set The 14k yellow gold case is in very good condition, showing sharp edges and the original brushed and polished finishes. The red LED display is crisp and fully functional, including the date feature. Crucially, the watch retains its original leather strap and 14k gold clasp, which still houses the original magnet used to set the time—a specific and often lost component of the Pulsar user experience. Mechanically, the quartz LED movement is running strong after passing our test bench, keeping accurate time as intended. The Ultimate Retro Tech Conversation Piece This is a watch for the collector who values the "hero" pieces of horological history. It is a rare opportunity to own a solid gold digital pioneer with the added provenance of a Tiffany & Co. signature. While modern smartwatches are disposable, the P3 remains a permanent statement of 1970s optimism and luxury. It works as a singular, bold accessory that stands out in any room, offering a level of historical cool that few traditional mechanical watches can replicate.

title: Patek Philippe 3571 - Blue Linen Dial
description: A Refined Expression Of The Seventies The 1970s was a period where Patek Philippe focused heavily on the continuity of design between the case and the bracelet. This reference 3491/3G is a product of that era, featuring a rectangular footprint that transitions into an 18k white gold integrated bracelet. The blue textured dial adds a layer of character that breaks up the monochromatic look of the white gold. It represents a time when the brand was moving toward more geometric and integrated forms, creating watches that felt like singular objects of jewelry. Significant Texture And Material Depth While many dress watches of the era relied on simple finishes, the textured blue dial on this reference provides a depth that changes depending on the light. The integrated bracelet is a masterclass in gold work, providing a fluid fit that follows the contours of the wrist. This configuration represents a more modern approach to luxury from a decade known for bold experimentation. It is a watch that relies on the balance of its proportions and the quality of its materials rather than unnecessary complexity. Good Condition With Original Integrity The 18k white gold case and the integrated bracelet are in good shape, retaining the original finish and sharp edges that define the reference. Both the crystal and the blue dial are well-preserved and free of significant flaws. The bracelet shows no excessive wear and remains structurally sound. After passing our test bench, the movement is running strong. A Discrete Alternative For The Patek Collector This is an ideal choice for someone who values the discretion of white gold but wants the substantial feel of an all-gold Patek. It sits flat on the wrist and offers a sophisticated look that is less common than the standard round dress watches of the period. It works as a foundational piece for a collection that prioritizes design history and material value. It remains a very wearable and sharp example of high-level Swiss craftsmanship.

title: Mirexal Superautomatic Diver
description: Here's a vintage Mirexal Superautomatic Diver 300 meter automatic with a two-tone black and blue dial from the 1970s. Keeps great time and is fully functional. Measurements include a 40mm case size and 18mm lug width. It's on a blue vintage-style leather rally strap. Bi-directional steel rotating bezel. Black dial with red and blue instrumentation at 3 o'clock. The lume in both the hands and hour markers developed an even yellow patina. This piece wears quite large, especially for its time, as the lugs are long and thick. Stainless steel case and caseback. Orange hands. Both the dial and crystal are free from any scratches or marks. All parts are original, including the crown, dial, movement, and caseback. Both the dial and case are in great cosmetic, untouched condition. Ready to wear!

title: Jean Ete - Lapis & Nephrite Jade
description: The Floral Stone Masterpiece Jean Ete represents a specific pocket of 1970s jewelry-watch history where the rules of traditional horology were secondary to the art of stone-setting. This 32mm 18k yellow gold piece is a definitive expression of that era. The dial is a rich slab of Lapis Lazuli, but the real focal point is the bezel, which features alternating stones of Lapis and Nephrite Jade. The deep blue of the Lapis paired with the soft, waxy green of the Jade creates a floral, organic aesthetic that is incredibly rare to find in vintage watches. It is an object designed for maximum visual impact, using natural materials to create a wearable piece of art. A Masterclass In Integrated Textures The design achieves total visual unity through its integrated 18k yellow gold woven bracelet. The bracelet isn't just a strap; it’s a continuation of the floral, decorative theme, flowing seamlessly into the gold case. This "unibody" construction was the peak of 1970s luxury, requiring significant hand-finishing to ensure the texture and taper were perfect. The result is a watch that wears more like a heavy gold cuff than a timepiece. It is an elegant, feminine execution that sits low and flush against the arm, highlighting the contrast between the vibrant stonework and the radiant yellow gold. Honest Character And Vibrant Stones The 18k yellow gold case and integrated bracelet are in very good condition, showing sharp lines and clear definition in the gold work. The Lapis Lazuli dial is clean and vibrant, free of any cracks. On the bezel, the stone setting remains impressive, though there is a small chip where a stone is missing at the 10 o’clock position; we have left this as-found to preserve the watch's honest history. The bracelet has been previously sized to a smaller fit. Mechanically, the watch is in great running condition after passing by our test bench, and is ready for it's new caretaker. A Distinctive Piece Of Jewelry History This is a watch for the individual who views timekeeping as secondary to personal style. It is a bold, high-level piece of 1970s jewelry that works perfectly as a standalone statement on the wrist. Whether for a lady or a collector who appreciates the avant-garde "floral" era of stone watches, this Jean Ete offers a combination of Lapis, Jade, and gold that is practically impossible to replicate. It is a rare, vibrant outlier that remains as elegant and striking as it was fifty years ago.

title: Helvetia Diver - Orange Diver
description: Here's a vintage Helvetia Diver automatic with a two-tone orange and silver dial from the 1970s. Keeps great time and is fully functional. Measurements include a 40mm case size and 20 mm lug width. It's on a black vintage-style leather rally strap. Bi-directional steel rotating bezel. Black dial with red and blue instrumentation at 3 o'clock. The lume in both the hands and the hour markers developed an even yellow patina. This piece wears quite large, especially for its time, as the lugs are long and thick. Stainless steel case and caseback. Both the dial and crystal are free from any scratches or marks. All parts are original, including the crown, dial, movement, and caseback. Both the dial and case are in great cosmetic, untouched condition. Ready to wear!

title: Glycine Compressor - NOS Condition
description: Here's a vintage Glycine compressor diver automatic with a blue vignette dial from the 1970s. New old stock condition. Keeps great time and is fully functional. Measurements include a 40mm case size and 20 mm lug width. Non-quickset date function at 3 o'clock. It's on a black alligator leather strap. The lume in both the hands and the hour markers developed an even yellow patina. Original brushed finishing on the bezel remained intact. This piece wears quite large, especially for its time as the lugs are long and thick. Stainless steel case and caseback. Both the dial and crystal are free from any scratches or marks. All parts are original, including the crown, dial, movement, and caseback. Both the dial and case are in great cosmetic, untouched condition. Ready to wear!

title: Audemars Piguet Ultra Thin Automatic - Hobnail Finish
description: Presenting the Clous De Paris Ultra Thin The 1970s was a decade defined by texture, and this Audemars Piguet Ultra Thin is a masterclass in the "Clous de Paris" or hobnail finish. While many dress watches from this era rely on high-polish surfaces, this reference uses a repetitive, pyramid-like engraving that covers the dial and the bezel. It is a design choice that effectively kills glare and gives the 18k white gold a deep, shimmering luster that changes depending on the angle of the light. It represents a period when AP was focusing on ultra-thin manual movements to create watches that felt more like a second skin than a piece of equipment. A Study In White Gold Uniformity The appeal of this specific reference lies in its total visual continuity. The hobnail pattern transitions seamlessly from the dial to the bezel, creating a unified face that makes the 32mm case feel larger and more intentional on the wrist. Housed in 18k white gold, the watch offers a monochromatic, architectural look that is significantly more discreet than its yellow gold counterparts. The integrated bracelet design ensures the watch sits perfectly flat, taking full advantage of the ultra-thin caliber housed within. It is a configuration for the collector who values the specific hand-finished techniques that defined mid-century Audemars Piguet. Excellent Preservation And Extended Length The 18k white gold case is in excellent condition, with the hobnail engraving remaining sharp and defined on both the bezel and the dial. The integrated white gold bracelet is equally well-preserved and carries a substantial weight, bringing the total mass of the watch to 82.1 grams. This Bracelet is still quite long which cannot be said for all integrated models we stock. Mechanically, the movement is running strong after passing our test bench, showing healthy amplitude and consistent timing. A Textured Alternative To The Icon This is a watch for the person who appreciates the thinness and craftsmanship of a Royal Oak but wants something more refined and rare. It is a piece of 1970s high-design that functions as a serious dress watch while maintaining enough texture to work in a casual setting. It is a heavy, high-quality object that represents the peak of Audemars Piguet’s non-sporting heritage. With its unique finish and generous bracelet length, it is a wearable piece of horological history that stands out through its subtlety.

title: Audemars Piguet - Pave Dial & Diamond Bezel
description: Diamonds Diamonds Diamonds on me dancing (that one Drake song) This vintage Audemars Piguet stands apart immediately for one reason: the factory diamond-set bezel , totaling approximately 3 carats . While diamond dials appear across various AP references from the era, a diamond bezel of this scale places the watch in a far more significant category. This was a top-tier option at the time, reflected in its very high original retail price and limited production. Perhaps the original owners were those pioneers in tech who are well known today. What makes this watch truly compelling is the combination: The combination of the 3-carat diamond bezel , the uncommon white gold execution , the pave diamond dial with sapphire markers sets this piece leagues ahead of most pieces AP was putting out at the time. The watch features a compact yet confident case, measuring approximately 30x33mm, paired with an integrated white-gold bracelet that fits wrists up to 6.75 inches . The bracelet has developed a subtle yellow hue over time, a result of aging white gold. This can be professionally rhodium plated upon request to return it to its original bright finish. Condition throughout is strong and honest. All parts are 100% original , including the signed crown, dial, movement, and caseback. Nothing has been altered or swapped, and the watch presents exactly as it should for a piece of this caliber. A TRUE COLLECTOR’S PIECE This is not just a product of Audemars Piguet’s history, it’s a well-preserved, seldom-seen example of the brand’s high-jewelry output at its peak. It works as an elegant statement for formal settings, yet remains striking and confident enough to elevate everyday wear. Versatile, bold, and unmistakably AP. Perfect whether you’re a seasoned collector or searching for a first truly special Audemars Piguet.

title: Audemars Piguet Decagon - 18K White Gold
description: The Silver Glitter Integrated Audemars Piguet In the late 1970s, Audemars Piguet was the definitive leader in the ultra-thin dress watch category, prioritizing material texture over technical bulk. This 31mm reference is a rare departure from the standard sunburst finishes of the era, featuring a "Silver Glitter" dial and a Decagon 10 sided case. The surface has a fine, granular texture that catches the light in a scattered manor, offering a dynamic visual depth that standard dials lack. It represents a period when AP was experimenting with unconventional dial and case finishes to elevate their already refined white gold silhouettes. A Masterclass In Two Tone Integration While most integrated AP dress watches are monochromatic, this example features a sophisticated two-tone execution. The case and the heavily textured woven bracelet are crafted in 18k white gold, but the bezel is rendered in 18k yellow gold. This creates a sharp, intentional frame around the glitter dial, pulling the warmth of the yellow gold into an otherwise cool, technical palette. The bracelet is a highlight of 1970s craftsmanship, flowing seamlessly from the case as a single, continuous object. At 76 grams, it carries the substantial weight of high-tier vintage jewelry while maintaining the low profile of an ultra-thin manual-wind movement. Excellent Case Definition And Honest Patina The 18k white and yellow gold components are in great shape, appearing well-preserved with sharp lines and clear definition in the woven bracelet links. The silver glitter dial is clean across the center, showing a light, honest patina around the extreme edges that adds a layer of vintage character without distracting from the overall aesthetic. The integrated bracelet is tight and maintains its structural integrity, and intricate woven design. Mechanically, the manual-wind movement is running strong after passing our test bench. A Distinctive Neo Vintage Statement This is a watch for the collector who appreciates the "unibody" design of 1970s Audemars Piguet but wants a configuration that stands apart from the common single-metal examples. The combination of the glitter dial and the two-tone white and yellow gold construction makes it a sophisticated outlier in the AP archive. It works perfectly as a unique formal piece or a high-end daily accessory, providing a level of hand-finished detail that modern manufacturing simply does not produce. It is a rare, high-quality object that captures the optimistic luxury of its era.

title: Croton Nivada Grenchen Chronograph
description: Here's a vintage Croton Chronomaster Aviator Sea Diver chronograph manual wind with a matte black dial from the late 60s, early 70s. Keeps great time and is fully functional. Measurements include a 38mm case size and 20mm lug width. It's on a brown vintage style leather strap. Valjoux movement (2 register) Features include the 30 minute counter at 3 a clock and the bi-directional rotating black bezel. Black dial with red and blue instrumentation at 3 a clock. The lume in both the hands and hour markers developed an even yellow patina. This piece wears quite large especially for its time as the lugs are long and thick. Stainless steel case and caseback. Scratches on the black bezel as seen in photos. Both the dial and crystal are free from any scratches or marks. All parts are original including the crown, dial, movement, and caseback. Both the dial and case are in great cosmetic, untouched condition. Ready to wear!

title: Audemars Piguet - Puzzle Bracelet
description: An Architecturally Unique Ellipse The reference 4647/903 is a rare departure from the traditional Ellipse form, earning its "Puzzle" nickname from the intricate construction of its 18k white gold case and matching bracelet. While the Ellipse silhouette is a staple of 20th-century dress watches, Audemars Piguet executed this version with a distinctive tiered, interlocking geometry that adds a layer of structural depth. The grey dial is kept clean and minimalist, allowing the focus to remain entirely on the play of light across the white gold surfaces. It is a design that feels architectural without being heavy, representing the height of AP's neo-vintage dress watch era. A Study In White Gold Integration The defining feature of this piece is the seamless transition from the sculptural case to the fully integrated 18k white gold bracelet. This is not a watch on a strap; it is a singular, continuous piece of jewelry designed to wrap around the wrist with total fluidity. The woven texture of the bracelet is tight and substantial, providing a tactile experience that modern luxury watches rarely replicate. At 95.6 grams, the watch carries a significant physical weight that confirms its status as a top-tier catalog offering from the 1990s. It is a configuration for the collector who values the "if you know, you know" prestige of white gold over the overt flash of yellow gold. Exceptional Preservation And Density This example is in excellent condition, showing sharp definition across the stepped case and no significant softening of the puzzle-like edges. The integrated bracelet is remarkably tight and well-preserved, fitting a wrist up to 7 inches comfortably. The grey dial remains clean and free of oxidation, protected by a flawless crystal. Mechanically, the manual-wind movement is running strong after passing our test bench with healthy amplitude. At nearly 96 grams of 18k white gold, the material density alone makes this a serious object of value, but the condition of the integrated clasp and bracelet links suggests it was rarely worn and carefully stored. The Collector’s Neo-Vintage Outlier This is a watch for the person who wants the elegance of an Ellipse but finds the standard round or oval cases too common. It sits in a unique niche of Audemars Piguet’s history, predating the brand's total pivot to the Royal Oak and highlighting their once-dominant position in the world of high-concept dress watches. It is a sophisticated, monochromatic statement that works perfectly under a cuff or as a standout daily accessory. Rare, heavy, and intentionally designed, it remains one of the most interesting non-sports watches in the AP archive.

title: 1974 Rolex Lady OP 6916 - Grey Dial
description: The Everyday Oyster Classic The Rolex Oyster Perpetual Date reference 6916 is the quintessential vintage daily driver. While many smaller Rolex models from the 1970s opt for the high-polish finish of a fluted bezel and Jubilee bracelet, this 26mm execution features a smooth bezel and an Oyster bracelet. It is a more utilitarian, focused aesthetic that highlights the classic proportions of the Oyster case. The grey dial offers a neutral, modern alternative to the more common silver or champagne options, providing a monochromatic look that has aged exceptionally well. Practicality In A Smaller Profile This reference represents a specific era where Rolex perfected the balance between luxury and durability in their smaller-format watches. The 6916 utilizes a high-beat automatic movement and an Oyster case that feels as robust as its larger counterparts. By pairing the smooth bezel with a grey dial, the watch avoids the "jewelry" feel of typical mid-century ladies' watches, leaning instead into a tool-watch identity that works across any wardrobe. It is a configuration for someone who wants the reliability and status of a vintage Rolex in a package that is discreet and easy to wear. Sharp Steel And Fresh Clarity The stainless steel case and Oyster bracelet are in good shape, retaining clear lines and a consistent finish. The grey dial is well-preserved, maintaining its deep slate tone with clean printing and crisp markers. The bracelet is original but is very tight for it's age, a plus for daily use. Mechanically, the watch is running strong after passing our test bench, showing healthy amplitude and keeping time as a reliable daily should. A Reliable Vintage Essential This is the watch for the individual who needs one high-quality piece to live on the wrist. It is a durable, automatic Rolex that doesn't demand attention but rewards it upon closer inspection. With its neutral grey palette and robust Oyster construction, it bridges the gap between a vintage collectible and a practical modern accessory. It is a classic 26mm timepiece that remains as functional and relevant today as it was in 1974.

title: 1993 Rolex Datejust 16233 - Diamond Dial
description: The Definitive Two Tone Execution The 16233 represents the era where the Datejust became a truly modern daily wearer. By combining the classic 36mm proportions with a sapphire crystal and the quickset automatic movement, Rolex created a watch that retains its vintage charm while offering complete technical reliability. The champagne dial serves as the perfect tonal match for the 18k yellow gold fluted bezel, creating the most recognizable silhouette in the history of the brand. Factory Diamond Sophistication The addition of factory diamond hour markers shifts this watch into a different category of luxury. While a standard stick dial is utilitarian, the stones provide a level of light play and depth that makes the dial feel far more substantial. It is an intentional configuration that prioritizes presence. Because the diamonds are factory set, the proportions and settings are exact, maintaining the clean lines of the champagne face without the visual clutter of aftermarket modifications. Sharp Case And Original Integrity This example is in great overall condition and maintains all of its original matching components. The case is sharp and the 18k yellow gold fluted bezel remains crisp and well defined. The two tone Jubilee bracelet shows well and both the champagne dial and sapphire crystal are clean and free of marks. After passing through our test bench, the automatic movement is running strong and is ready for regular use. It is a well maintained example that shows no signs of neglect. A Timeless piece ready for its' next caretaker This is the watch for the person who wants the quintessential Rolex experience. The two tone Datejust on a Jubilee bracelet is a design that has transcended trends and remains just as relevant today as it was in 1993. It carries a specific level of prestige and versatility, working just as well in a professional setting as it does in a casual one. The diamond dial adds just enough elevation to make it feel special without sacrificing its ability to be a daily driver.

title: Piaget Protocole 9154
description: Here's a vintage Piaget tank reference 9154 manual wind in 18K yellow gold with a champagne sunburst dial from the late 1970s, early 1980s. Recently serviced! Keeps great time and is fully functional. Measurements include a 27x25mm tank case size and 18mm lug width. It's on an aftermarket black leather strap. Time only. Gold case and screw down caseback. Caliber 19J mechanical movement. Bark finish on the bezel. The dial and crystal is free from any scratches or marks. All parts are original including the dial, crown, movement, and caseback. Both the dial and case are in great cosmetic, untouched condition. Ready to wear!

title: Piaget 9065 - Diamond, Lapis & Mother of Pearl Dial
description: The 83-Gram Lapis and Pearl Sculpture This Reference 9065 is a prime example of why Piaget owned the 1970s and 80s "Jet Set" aesthetic. It moves past standard horology into the realm of wearable art, featuring a high-contrast stone dial configuration that is rarely seen in the wild. Executed in solid 18k white gold, the watch carries a significant 83.3-gram heft, giving it a dense, substantial presence that belies its elegant 31.5mm proportions. A Study in Natural Contrast The centerpiece is the rare, factory-original multi-material dial. The deep, celestial blue of the Lapis Lazuli center is framed by a shimmering Mother of Pearl outer ring, creating a "target" effect that pulls the eye inward. This is further elevated by twelve factory-set Diamond indices that bridge the two materials. The silver dauphine hands keep the focus on the stones, resulting in a monochromatic white gold and blue palette that feels colder and more modern than the typical yellow gold Piaget's of the era. Integrated White Gold Unity The 18k white gold case and bracelet are treated as a single, uninterrupted unit. The integrated mesh bracelet features a complex woven texture that catches light from every angle, flowing seamlessly from the case without the break of traditional lugs. It fits up to a 7.6-inch wrist, secured by a signed clasp with screw adjustments. This design creates a "cuff" effect on the wrist, making the watch feel like a permanent piece of jewelry rather than an accessory. Excellent Condition & Integrity This example is a true survivor, presented in excellent, untouched cosmetic condition. Both the stone dial and the crystal are free from scratches, cracks, or marks—a critical detail for collectors of vintage stone dials where any hairline fracture can ruin the value. All components are original, including the crown, dial, and caseback. Mechanically, the manual-wind movement is fully functional and running strong after passing our test bench. It is a healthy, verified, and exceptionally rare artifact from Piaget’s most creative era. Key Specs Reference: 9065 D4 Year: 1980s Movement: Quartz Case Size: 31.5mm Metal: 18K White Gold Bracelet: 18K White Gold Integrated Bracelet Length: 7.6 inches Dial: Natural Lapis Lazuli & Mother-of-Pearl with Factory Diamond Indices Weight: 83.3g SKU: 818

title: Audemars Piguet 6005ST - Grey Tropical Dial
description: Here's a vintage Audemars Piguet Royal Oak reference 6005ST quartz in steel with a textured grey dial from the 1980s (B series) It keeps great time and is fully functional. Measurements include a 34mm case size. Lugs are integrated. Bracelet fits up to a 6.5 -inch wrist. Time only. Beautiful grey waffle-textured dial. This dial is an extremely rare variation made only for this reference with the hour markers going through every minute. Unpolished case and bracelet. Rectangle case. Matching gold hands and hour markers. Makes for a great Royal Oak alternative. All parts are original, including the crown, dial, movement, and caseback. Both the dial and case are in great cosmetic, untouched condition. Ready to wear!

title: 1976 Rolex Cellini 4311/8 - Integrated 18K YG Champagne Dial
description: Integrated Design From The Seventies The reference 4311 is a pure representation of the jewelry watch movement that defined its era. During this time, Rolex used the Cellini line to experiment with forms that sat outside the utilitarian Oyster case. This model features a square footprint that flows directly into a heavy integrated yellow gold bracelet. It is a design that prioritizes the continuity of metal, making the watch feel like a single solid object on the wrist rather than a case attached to a strap. Significant Presence In Solid Gold Beyond the design, this piece carries a weight that reinforces its status as a top tier luxury offering. The square proportions offer a visual footprint that feels substantial, providing a balanced look for a variety of wrist sizes. The champagne sunburst dial keeps the aesthetic monochromatic and focused, allowing the craftsmanship of the integrated gold work to be the primary focus of the piece. Excellent Condition With Natural Oxidation The yellow gold case and bracelet are in very good condition and show a natural oxidation that has developed into an attractive hue across the gold surfaces. This patina is a clear indicator of the age of the piece and suggests it has been preserved without aggressive polishing. The champagne sunburst dial is clean and free of damage. The bracelet is of a generous length and remains structurally sound. The manual wind movement is running strong after passing our test bench. A Sophisticated Choice For The Specialist This is for the collector who wants the material presence of an all gold Rolex in a more refined and geometric format. It works perfectly as a dedicated dress watch that carries a significant amount of material value and historical interest. It remains a confident and wearable piece from one of the most expressive decades of Swiss watchmaking.

title: Piaget Gouverneur - Tiffany Stamped
description: The Fifth Avenue Connection The Piaget Gouverneur 15988 is a masterclass in the traditional dress watch silhouette. This reference is defined by its stepped 18k yellow gold bezel and sculpted lugs, offering a level of detail that standard round watches often overlook. This particular example features a white dial with a Tiffany & Co. retailer stamp. Double-signed dials from this era represent a specific period of partnership between the Swiss manufacture and the iconic New York retailer. It is a configuration that adds a layer of provenance and collectability to an already refined design. A Significant Retailer Signature This watch is more than just a 1990s Piaget. The Tiffany & Co. signature is clean and properly aligned above the six o'clock position. While the Gouverneur series is appreciated for its ultra-thin automatic movements and classical proportions, the double stamp elevates it into the category of a true collector's piece. The contrast of the black Roman numerals and the crisp white dial maintains a formal aesthetic that remains relevant. It is a subtle nod to those who recognize the importance of retailer-signed pieces in the history of horology. Honest Condition And Verified Performance This example is in great overall condition and shows that it was well cared for by its previous owner. The 18k yellow gold case retains its sharp lines and the stepped bezel is well-defined. The dial and crystal are clean with no notable blemishes to report. It comes on its original leather strap with the matching 18k yellow gold Piaget buckle. The automatic movement is running strong after passing our test bench and the quick-set date functions as it should. The Collector's Daily Dress Watch This is the right choice for someone who values rarity that doesn't scream for attention. The Tiffany signature is a "if you know, you know" detail that sets this apart from standard Piaget production. It functions perfectly as a dedicated dress watch but the automatic movement provides a level of convenience that makes it an easy option for regular wear. It is a sophisticated, historical piece that offers a lot of value for someone looking to enter the world of double-signed vintage watches.

title: Omega Speedmaster 3510 - Milan Limited Edition
description: Here's an Omega Speedmaster automatic chronograph reference number 3510 with a black dial from 1999. Limited edition, issued 0548/1999, this Speedmaster marks the 100th year anniversary for a sporting event in Milan. Keeps great time and is fully functional. Measurements include a 39mm case and 18mm lug width. On its original Omega stamped bracelet that fits up to a 7.25 inch wrist. Black sub dials. Matching patina in both the hands and hour markers. The sub dials are for the seconds, 30 minute track, and 12 hour track. Brushed finishing on the sides of the case and bracelet. All parts are original including the signed Omega crown, caseback, dial, and movement. Both the dial and case are in great cosmetic, untouched condition. Ready to wear!

title: Patek Ellipse 3545 - Blue Linen Dial
description: The Textured Landscape Ellipse The Patek Philippe reference 3545/2 is a fascinating subversion of the brand’s most iconic shape. While the "Golden Ellipse" is traditionally oriented vertically, this "sideways" execution rotates the geometry 90 degrees, creating a wide, landscape profile that sits differently on the wrist than any other dress watch. This 1980s example is further distinguished by a rare textured blue dial, which replaces the standard flat sunburst with a deep, fabric-like finish. It is a configuration that captures the experimental spirit of Patek’s late-century design, prioritizing architectural form and material depth. A Masterclass in Integrated Gold The appeal of the 3545/2 lies in its total visual unity. The 18k yellow gold case is paired with a fully integrated bracelet, meaning the watch was engineered as a single, continuous gold sculpture. The bracelet is a highlight of Patek’s jewelry-era craftsmanship, featuring a tight, intricate weave that flows seamlessly from the horizontal case. Because it houses a manual-wind movement, the watch maintains a razor-thin profile, allowing the 92.7 grams of solid gold to wrap around the wrist with total fluidity. This is a watch designed for the collector who values the "unibody" aesthetic of the 1970s and 80s. Honest Character and Heavy Density The 18k yellow gold case is in honest condition, retaining its original proportions and showing a personalized engraving on the caseback—a classic touch of provenance. The rare blue textured dial has developed a unique patina, with age-related character marks that give it an organic, "lived-in" feel. The integrated bracelet remains in good shape, currently fitting a wrist up to 7 inches. Mechanically, the manual-wind movement is running well after passing our test bench, showing a strong 263 amplitude. At nearly 93 grams, the material density is immense, providing a tactile sense of quality that modern watches rarely match. The Sideways Patek Outlier This is a watch for the individual who appreciates the heritage of the Ellipse but wants a version that challenges the standard aesthetic. The horizontal orientation and the textured dial make it a significant outlier in the Patek Philippe catalog, offering a much bolder, more "TV-style" presence than the traditional oval. It is a heavy, substantial piece of vintage luxury that functions as both a serious design statement and a piece of historic jewelry. Rare, intentional, and physically impressive, it remains one of the most interesting variations in the Golden Ellipse lineage.

title: Audemars Piguet Cobra - Blue Diamond Dial
description: Presenting the Navy Diamond Baby Cobra The Audemars Piguet Baby Cobra is a definitive relic of the era when the brand dominated the "unibody" jewelry-watch category. While yellow gold was the standard for the 1970s, this B-series example is rendered in solid 18k white gold, offering a much cooler and more industrial aesthetic. The navy blue matte dial provides a deep, saturated contrast to the white gold case, a pairing that is significantly harder to find than the typical silver or champagne variants. It is a configuration that prioritizes color and texture, creating a high-contrast look that feels sharp and contemporary. High Jewelry Dial Architecture What elevates this specific reference is the factory diamond-set hour markers. In an era where most dress watches relied on simple printed indices, the use of brilliant-cut diamonds adds a structural depth to the navy dial, catching the light against the flat, matte finish. The 31x32mm "Cobra" silhouette remains one of the most aggressive shapes in the AP archive, featuring a seamless transition from the case to the heavily textured integrated bracelet. Because it utilizes an ultra-thin manual-wind movement, the watch maintains a remarkably low profile despite its significant material density. Preserved White Gold And Integrated Finish The 18k white gold case and integrated bracelet are in very good condition, retaining the deep, fabric-like texture that defines this series. The navy blue dial is clean and stable, providing a crisp backdrop for the diamond markers and white gold hands. The integrated bracelet is tight and maintains its original supple feel, currently sized to fit a wrist up to 6.75 inches. Mechanically, the manual-wind movement is running strong after passing our test bench, and at 86.1 grams, the watch has a serious, high-quality weight that confirms its status as a top-tier catalog piece from the early 80s. A Stealth Wealth Power Statement This is a watch for the collector who understands the historical importance of Audemars Piguet’s non-sports heritage but wants a piece that flies under the radar. The white gold construction makes it much more discreet than a yellow gold Cobra, while the navy and diamond dial ensures it remains a serious piece of design. It is a heavy, substantial object of luxury that works perfectly as a sophisticated daily outlier or a dedicated formal statement. It represents the peak of AP’s ability to merge high-jewelry materials with avant-garde geometry. Key Specs Model: Baby Cobra Year: Late 70s / Early 80s (B-Series) Movement: Manual Case Size: 31x32mm Bezel: 18k WG Bracelet: 18K WG Integrated Dial: Navy Blue Matte with Diamond Hours Wrist Size: 6.5–6.75in Metal: 18K WG Weight: 86.1g SKU: 230

title: Vacheron Constantin Patrimony - Skeleton Chronograph
description: A Masterclass in Hand-Finishing We are delighted to present this neo-vintage Vacheron Constantin Patrimony Chronograph Ref. 47100, a rare and striking creation from 1994. Crafted in 18k yellow gold and powered by a manual-wind chronograph movement, this reference embodies Vacheron’s devotion to haute horlogerie at the close of the 20th century. Case, Dial & Bracelet The watch features a 38mm yellow gold case, perfectly sized for modern wear yet retaining a timeless classical profile. Its skeletonized dial reveals the hand-finished chronograph movement beneath, showcasing intricate architecture and decorative artistry rarely seen. Applied gold hour markers and matching hands bring balance and legibility to this openworked design. It is fitted on its original gold bracelet, sized for wrists up to 7 inches, completing the seamless elegance of the piece. Rarity, Condition & Collectability Produced in fewer than 100 examples, the Ref. 47100 ranks among the most elusive neo-vintage chronographs ever created by Vacheron Constantin. Its skeletonized dial, exquisitely finished manual chronograph caliber, and limited production elevate it to the status of a true collectible. This example is accompanied by its complete original box and papers, underscoring its provenance and rarity. The case, crown, dial, movement, and caseback remain original and untouched, while the watch itself is preserved in excellent condition, both mechanically and cosmetically. Timeless Appeal At once technical and artistic, the Patrimony Ref. 47100 is more than a chronograph — it is a fusion of mechanical mastery and aesthetic daring, emblematic of 1990s watchmaking at its highest level. Elegant, bold, and versatile, it transitions effortlessly from refined formal wear to the collection cabinet of the world’s most discerning enthusiasts.

title: 1976 Rolex Cellini 4084 – Blue Vignette Dial
description: Deep Center Fading To Black The 4084 is a product of the mid-seventies, an era where Rolex moved the Cellini line into more experimental territory. The TV case shape offers a wide, rectangular presence that sits differently on the wrist than a standard circular watch. While it presents with the low-profile look of brushed stainless steel, the case is executed entirely in 18k white gold. It is a quiet material choice that prioritizes the owner's experience over outward flash, maintaining a tool-watch aesthetic in a precious metal format. A Deep Gradient Narrative The blue vignette dial is the focal point of this reference. It transitions from a bright cobalt center to a deep, near-black perimeter, a finishing technique that Rolex perfected during this period. This gradient adds a level of visual weight and motion that a flat dial cannot achieve. Paired with the brushed white gold case, the dial color pops with a specific intensity that feels intentional and structured. It represents a peak era of Rolex dial experimentation that remains highly sought after by those who value color depth. Preserved In Top Form This watch is in excellent condition and has been remarkably well-preserved over the last five decades. The white gold case maintains its sharp, brushed finish and shows no signs of heavy wear or softening. The blue vignette dial is in top form, showing no signs of spotting or age-related degradation. The crystal is clear. It is currently mounted on a clean leather strap with an aftermarket buckle. After passing our test bench, the manual-wind movement is running strong and is ready for regular use. A Discrete High Tier Choice This is for the person who wants a vintage Rolex that steps outside the traditional Oyster Perpetual shadow. It offers a sophisticated, geometric silhouette that feels more like a piece of intentional 1970s design than a standard timepiece. The combination of the brushed precious metal and the deep blue gradient dial makes it an easy choice for someone who values discretion and specific details. It is a heavy-hitting gold reference that stays under the radar until it is right in front of you.

title: Omega Speedmaster Triple Calender - Blue Dial
description: Here's an Omega Speedmaster reference number 3521.30 triple date chronograph with a blue dial from the late 90s, early 2000s. Keeps great time and is fully functional. Measurements include a 39mm case size and 18mm lug width. On its original Omega stamped bracelet that fits up to a 7 inch wrist. Features include the time, chronograph function, day, date, and month. Steel case and caseback. The lume in both the hands and lume plots near the hour markers developed a yellow patina. The sub dials developed some aging as seen in the photos. Both the dial and crystal are free of any scratches or marks. All parts are original including the signed Omega crown, caseback, dial, and movement. Both the dial and case are in great cosmetic, untouched condition. Ready to wear!

title: Cartier Tank Obus - 18K Yellow Gold
description: The Quadrant Enamel Classic The Cartier Tank Obus is a distinct departure from the elongated rectangles typically associated with the brand. This 1980s execution, often referred to as the "Quadrant," utilizes a perfectly square 20x20mm 18k yellow gold case. What sets this specific reference apart is the bezel; rather than leaving the gold plain, Cartier inlaid it with black enamel Roman numerals. This design choice pushes the hour markers to the bezel, allowing the white dial to remain open and minimalist. It is a sophisticated, geometric take on the Tank lineage that feels more like a piece of stationary architecture than a standard timepiece A Rare Geometric Execution In the world of vintage Cartier, the Obus occupies a niche space for collectors who appreciate "shaped" watches. The square proportions give it a stout presence on the wrist despite its refined dimensions. The contrast between the high-polish 18k yellow gold and the crisp black enamel creates a graphic intensity that is signature to Cartier’s early 20th-century design language, revived here for the 1980s. It is a configuration that prioritizes the case's silhouette, making the bezel the primary dial while the internal white face serves as a clean, secondary backdrop. Sharp Gold And Preserved Enamel The 18k yellow gold case is in good condition, maintaining its sharp square corners and the distinct "bullet" lugs that give the Obus its name. Crucially, the black enamel Roman numerals on the bezel are well-preserved, retaining their deep pigment and clean edges. The white dial is excellent, showing no signs of spotting or degradation. The watch is currently fitted to a leather strap with an aftermarket buckle. Mechanically, the watch is being sorted for a fresh battery as part of our intake process runs great. A Sophisticated Square Alternative This is a watch for the Cartier enthusiast who already owns a Tank Louis or a Santos and wants something more obscure. The Tank Obus offers a level of vintage charm and geometric character that is hard to find in modern catalogs. It functions as a formal dress watch with a heavy lean toward jewelry, offering a look that is both historically grounded and visually unique. It is a rare, intentional design that remains one of the most interesting "hidden" references in the Cartier archive.

title: Audemars Piguet – TV Case
description: Here’s a vintage Audemars Piguet in 18k yellow gold automatic with a black dial from the 1980s. Measurements include a 34mm TV case and 18mm lug width. On an aftermarket black leather strap. Time only. Matching gold hands and hour markers (dauphine hands). Braided finish on the bezel. All parts including the crown, dial, movement, and caseback are original. Ready to wear!

title: Cartier Santos 2823 – White Roman Dial
description: Here's a vintage Cartier Santos steel automatic reference 2823 with a white roman numeral dial from the 90s. Keeps great time and is fully functional. Measurements include a 32mm case. Integrated bracelet with screws that fit up to a 7 inch wrist. Blue cabochon crown. Silver dial with blue hands. Matching black roman numerals and minute track. Blued hands. Quickset date function between 4-5 a clock. Stainless steel case and caseback. Nice brushed finishing on the bracelet. Drilled lug holes. Makes for a great watch for both men and women. A nice thin profile. Hard to come by automatic Santos with white dials. All the parts are original including the crown, dial, movement, and caseback. Both the dial and case are in great cosmetic, untouched condition. Ready to wear!

title: Rolex Oysterdate Precision 6694 – Silver Dial
description: Early Oysterdate, pure and utilitarian This Rolex Oysterdate Precision from the 60s is a clean example of the model in its earlier, more stripped-back form. Manual wind, no frills, and built around clarity and proportion, it reflects Rolex’s everyday tool-watch philosophy before automatics became the norm. But this dial boasts something not often seen. Why this configuration works Early 6694s like this one carry a different character than later examples. The dagger indices and alpha hands give the dial more elegant look, while the date wheel provides utility. At 34mm, the proportions remain ideal for daily wear, sitting comfortably and reading cleanly on the wrist. It’s a watch defined by restraint rather than complication. Condition, clearly stated This one is in really good overall shape for the age. The case shows wear consistent with a watch that’s been worn, not overcorrected. The dial presents with dagger indices intact, and the hands show matching patina, which is consistent with age. Mechanically, the movement is running and keeping time well after passing by our test bench. All core components, case, dial, movement, and crown remain original. A straightforward vintage Rolex This isn’t about rarity or perfection. It’s about an honest early Oysterdate that captures why manual-wind Rolex watches remain so appealing decades later. For someone looking for a simple, historically grounded Rolex with clear design language and everyday wearability, this ref. 6694 delivers exactly that, a no-nonsense classic from an earlier chapter of the brand’s history.

title: 1993 Rolex Datejust 16233 – Champagne Diamond Dial
description: An Essential Collection Staple The 16233 is the bridge between vintage charm and modern reliability. By 1993, Rolex had perfected the Datejust formula by pairing the classic 36mm proportions with a scratch-resistant sapphire crystal and the high-beat 3135 caliber. This execution features the definitive combination: a champagne dial that seamlessly matches the 18k yellow gold fluted bezel. It is a design that has transcended trends to become the most recognizable luxury watch silhouette in the world. Elevated With Factory Diamonds While a standard Datejust is a masterclass in utility, the addition of factory diamond hour markers shifts this piece into a higher category of prestige. Because these stones are set by Rolex in-house, the proportions and settings are exact, offering a level of light-play and depth that a standard stick dial cannot match. It provides just enough flash to make the watch feel like a special-occasion piece while remaining subtle enough to function as a dependable daily driver. Sharp Integrity And Matching Components This 1993 example is in great overall condition, maintaining all of its original matching components. The stainless steel case remains sharp, and the 18k yellow gold fluted bezel is well-defined with crisp edges. The two-tone Jubilee bracelet shows well, offering the comfort and fluidity that made this reference a global standard. After passing through our test bench, the automatic movement is running strong with a healthy amplitude, showing it has been well-maintained over the years. A Permanent Icon For The Daily Rotation This is the watch for the person who wants the "one-watch" Rolex experience. The 16233 on a Jubilee bracelet is a design that is as relevant today as it was thirty years ago, working just as effectively with a suit as it does with a casual weekend look. The diamond dial adds a layer of sophistication that separates it from the pack, making it a high-value entry into the world of precious metal Rolex. It remains an honest, high-performing example of the most successful watch design ever made.

title: Piaget Integrated 18K WG - Lapis Lazuli Dial
description: The Intersection Of Stone And White Gold Piaget defined 1980s luxury through an obsession with natural materials and ultra-thin watchmaking. This reference 9286 A6 is a prime example, featuring a deep blue lapis lazuli dial that provides a depth of color impossible to achieve with paint. The integrated 18k white gold bracelet transitions seamlessly into the case, turning the watch into a single, cohesive piece of jewelry. While yellow gold was the standard of the era, white gold offers a more understated and monochromatic look that allows the natural character of the stone dial to take center stage. Significant Craftsmanship And Material Depth The use of lapis lazuli is a hallmark of the highest tier of Piaget's jewelry era, where each dial is unique due to the natural variations in the stone. The integrated bracelet is a masterclass in gold work, providing a fluid fit that follows the contours of the wrist and gives the piece substantial presence. This configuration represents the brand at its most confident, combining a world-class manual wind movement with materials that carry significant intrinsic value and historical weight. Very Good Condition With Honest Details The 18k white gold case and integrated bracelet are in very good condition, showing a substantial weight and sharp original finishing throughout. There is some minor yellowing on the back of the case which is expected for white gold of this era and is not visible when the watch is worn. The lapis dial is well-preserved, though a few small specks are visible on the surface of the stone under scrutiny. The manual wind movement is running strong after passing our test bench. A Sophisticated Statement Piece This is a sophisticated choice for the collector who values the era of stone-dial Piagets but wants something more unique than common yellow gold references. The white gold construction gives it a stealthy presence while the lapis dial provides a vibrant pop of color. It is a sculptural timekeeper that works perfectly as a refined statement piece today. It remains a wearable and historically significant example of the most creative period in the history of the brand.

title: Omega Speedmaster 3513 – Black Dial
description: Here's a vintage Omega Speedmaster reference 3513 automatic with a black dial from the 1990s. Measurements include a 39mm case and 18mm lug width. Bracelet fits up to 7 inch wrist. All parts including the crown, dial, movement, and caseback are original. Ready to wear!

title: Cartier Santos 2961 – White Roman Dial
description: Here's a vintage Cartier Santos reference 2961 two tone with a white Roman numeral dial from the 1980s. Measurements include a 29x41mm case and the bracelet fits up to a 7 inch wrist. All parts including the crown, dial, movement, and caseback are original. Ready to wear!

title: 1973 Rolex Oysterdate Precision 6694 – Vertical Stripe Dial
description: Engineered to be focused on precision The 6694 OysterDate remains the purest connection to Rolex’s mechanical roots. As a manual-wind reference, it sports a significantly thinner case profile that sits low and flat against the wrist. It is a watch that requires a daily ritual—a morning wind that connects the wearer to the movement. This 34mm Oyster case offers a lean, focused silhouette that emphasizes the "Precision" nature of its mid-century engineering. Architectural Horizontal Brushing While most silver dials from this era utilize a vertical or sunburst finish, this example features a rare horizontal brushed texture. This creates a distinct metallic, architectural feel that catches light in a linear fashion, separating it from more common production dials. Honest Vintage Character This piece is in good overall condition and wears its history openly. The stainless steel case and Oyster bracelet show the normal surface wear and light marks consistent with a watch that has been worn and enjoyed since the early 1970s. The dial is in good shape. The acrylic crystal has light surface scratching. After passing our test bench, the manual movement is running strong with a healthy amplitude, maintaining the mechanical integrity expected of the reference. The Essential Vintage Daily The 6694 is often the gateway into vintage Rolex for a reason: it is indestructible, classically proportioned, and mechanically straightforward. The 34mm size remains a benchmark for those who prefer a discreet but confident presence on the wrist. With its unique horizontally brushed dial and classic Oyster bracelet, this example offers a specific character that stands out from the standard vintage crowd. It is a balanced, high-utility timepiece that remains as functional today as it was fifty years ago.

title: Rolex Oysterdate Precision 6694 – Vertical Stripe Dial
description: Here's a vintage Rolex Oysterdate Precision reference 6694 manual wind from the 1970s with a silver vertical stripe dial. Measurements include a 34mm steel case and 19mm lug width. On a heavy oyster steel bracelet. Nonquick date function at 3 o'clock. Bracelet fits up to a 7 inch wrist. All parts including the crown, dial, movement, and caseback are original. Ready to wear!

title: 1969 Rolex Oysterdate Precision 6694 – Silver Linen Dial
description: Linen texture, everyday Rolex This Rolex Oysterdate Precision from 1969 is a straightforward, well-balanced example of Rolex’s manual-wind era. Compact, legible, and purpose-built, it’s the kind of watch that defined daily wear before automatic movements became the default. The silver linen dial is the defining detail subtle, textured, and unmistakably period-correct. Why this configuration works The linen dial adds depth without turning the watch into a statement piece. Its crosshatch texture catches light softly and gives the dial more presence than a flat silver execution, while still keeping everything calm and readable. Light spotting is present, but it isn’t immediately noticeable and stays consistent with the watch’s age. Paired with the 34mm steel case and smooth bezel, the watch feels balanced and intentional. The manual-wind movement reinforces that simplicity — no excess, just a clean, functional layout that wears easily. Condition, clearly stated Condition is strong for its age. The case shows wear consistent with careful use, the bracelet remains tight for its vintage, and the crystal is clean. The dial presents well overall, with its patina adding character rather than distraction. All major components are original. The movement is running in great shape after coming from our test bench. A classic Oysterdate with character This isn’t a flashy Rolex or a rarity play. It’s a well-preserved Oysterdate that highlights why these watches remain so wearable decades later — simple design, thoughtful proportions, and a dial that rewards a closer look. A solid vintage Rolex for someone who values texture, history, and an honest, everyday piece that still feels right on the wrist.

title: 1963 Rolex Oysterdate Precision 6694 – Silver Dial
