Manual wind, no frills, and built around clarity and proportion, it reflects Rolex’s everyday tool-watch philosophy before automatics became the norm. But this dial boasts something not often seen. Why this configuration works Early 6694s like this one carry a different character than later examples. The dagger indices and alpha hands give the dial more elegant look, while the date wheel provides utility. At 34mm, the proportions remain ideal for daily wear, sitting comfortably and reading cleanly on the wrist. It’s a watch defined by restraint rather than complication. Condition, clearly stated This one is in really good overall shape for the age. The case shows wear consistent with a watch that’s been worn, not overcorrected. The dial presents with dagger indices intact, and the hands show matching patina, which is consistent with age. Mechanically, the movement is running and keeping time well after passing by our test bench. All core components, case, dial, movement, and crown remain original. A straightforward vintage Rolex This isn’t about rarity or perfection. It’s about an honest early Oysterdate that captures why manual-wind Rolex watches remain so appealing decades later. For someone looking for a simple, historically grounded Rolex with clear design language and everyday wearability, this ref. 6694 delivers exactly that, a no-nonsense classic from an earlier chapter of the brand’s history.

title: Rolex Oysterdate Precision 6694 – Silver Dial
description: Here's a vintage Rolex Oysterdate Precision reference 6694 manual wind from the 1970s with a silver dial. Measurements include a 34mm steel case and 19mm lug width. Non quick date function at 3 o'clock. Bracelet fits up to a 7 inch wrist. Matching patina in both the hands and hour markers. Alpha hands. All parts including the crown, dial, movement, and caseback are original. Ready to wear!

title: 1972 Rolex Oysterdate Precision 6694 – Silver Dial
description: A straightforward Oysterdate, done the right way This Rolex Oysterdate Precision ref. 6694 from 1972 is exactly what the model was meant to be: simple, legible, and built for everyday wear. With its silver sunburst dial and manual-wind movement, it reflects a time when Rolex focused on clarity and reliability rather than excess. It’s familiar in the best sense — honest and well proportioned. Why this configuration works The silver sunburst dial gives the watch just enough texture to avoid feeling flat, catching light subtly while keeping the overall look clean. Paired with the 34mm steel case and smooth bezel, the result is balanced and easy to wear, sitting comfortably between dressy and utilitarian. The manual-wind movement and non-quickset date keep things straightforward, reinforcing the Oysterdate’s role as a practical daily Rolex rather than a statement piece. Condition, clearly stated Condition is consistent with age and use. The case shows normal wear, and the bracelet remains solid and wearable for its vintage. The dial displays spotting throughout, visible on close inspection and reflective of natural aging. The crystal is clean, and the buckle presents well for its age. Mechanically, the watch is running and performing respectably, with stable timekeeping and healthy amplitude for a piece of this era. All major components — case, dial, movement, and crown — are original. A classic manual-wind Rolex This isn’t a rare or exotic Rolex, and it doesn’t need to be. It’s a dependable Oysterdate that highlights why these watches remain so appealing decades later: good design, sensible proportions, and an easy presence on the wrist. For someone looking for an honest vintage Rolex that can be worn regularly without fuss, this ref. 6694 delivers exactly that.

title: 1976 Rolex Cellini – Champagne Dial
description: The Geometric Side Of Rolex Elegance While the Oyster case defines Rolex’s utility, the Cellini line is where the brand explores pure formal aesthetics. The 3811, dating to 1974, is a quintessential mid-century dress watch, utilizing a 23mm square case that offers a sharp, geometric alternative to the standard rounded silhouette. Executed in 18k yellow gold, it carries the warmth and prestige of a precious metal Rolex while maintaining a slim, low profile that can only be achieved with a manual-wind movement. Sunburst Depth And Organic Patina The champagne sunburst dial is the perfect tonal match for the 18k gold case, creating a monochromatic look that radiates with light. On this specific example, the indices have developed a subtle, organic patina over the years that turned them a darker, bronzed character that contrasts beautifully against the radiant champagne dial. It is a refined, "if you know, you know" detail that highlights the piece's vintage authenticity. Excellent Form And Mechanical Health This Cellini is in very good condition, with the 18k gold case retaining its sharp edges and proper proportions. The dial and crystal are clean, free of the scratches or environmental damage often seen in non-Oyster vintage pieces. It is currently mounted on a fresh leather strap with an aftermarket buckle, allowing the gold case to take center stage. Mechanically, the manual-wind movement is running exceptionally after passing by our test bench, proving that Rolex’s dress-tier engineering is as durable as its sport-tier counterparts. A Sophisticated, Minimalist Statement This is the choice for the collector who values discretion and historical design. The 23mm square proportions make it a highly versatile unisex piece, wearing with a presence that feels more like a piece of high-end jewelry than a bulky timepiece. It is an honest, well-preserved example of a golden era for Rolex, offering a level of sophistication that stays quietly under the radar.

title: Cartier Santos Galbée LM – Stainless Steel, White Roman Dial
description: The Evolution of the Galbée The Santos Galbée Reference 1564 represents the refined evolution of the world's first pilot watch. Introduced in the late 1980s and perfected throughout the 1990s, the "Galbée" (meaning "curved" or "contoured") case was Cartier's response to the boxy silhouettes of the previous decade. By curving the case and lugs to follow the natural profile of the arm, Cartier turned a rugged industrial icon into a masterclass in ergonomics. This 29mm x 41mm stainless steel specimen offers a sleek, monochromatic profile that defines the understated luxury of the period. Clinical Cartier Design The dial is the quintessential Cartier signature featuring a crisp white field with bold black Roman numerals and the iconic railway minute track. Paired with blued-steel sword hands and the characteristic sapphire cabochon crown, the aesthetic is clinical yet undeniably elegant. The 1564 is a purely architectural piece, stripped of gold accents to let the interplay of brushed and polished steel surfaces do the talking. It is a design that bridges the gap between a utilitarian tool watch and a formal dress piece, making it one of the most versatile references in the entire Santos lineage. Condition and Integrity This example is preserved in great cosmetic condition and retains all its original components, including the dial, hands, cabochon crown, and caseback. The stainless steel bracelet is remarkably well-maintained and features the modern butterfly clasp with a total of 22 links, comfortably accommodating up to a 7-inch wrist. The brushed surfaces of the case remain sharp, and the polished bezel screws provide that characteristic high-contrast shine that makes the Santos immediately recognizable from across a room. Performance Verified Mechanically, the Cartier quartz movement is performing exactly as intended and has been fully verified for long-term reliability after passing our test bench. The 1564 is favored by collectors for its "set-it-and-forget-it" convenience, offering the legendary Santos silhouette without the specialized maintenance requirements of vintage mechanical calibers. This is a healthy, verified, and ready-to-wear artifact from Cartier’s modern golden era, representing a timeless investment in design purity. Key Specs Reference: 1564 Model: Santos Galbée LM (Large Model) Era: 1990s Movement: Cartier High-Precision Quartz Case Size: 29mm x 41mm Bracelet: Stainless Steel (20 Links, Butterfly Clasp) Bracelet Length: 6.5 inches Dial: White with Black Roman Numerals Metal: Stainless Steel SKU: 183

title: Universal Genève Polerouter – Tropical Dial
description: Here's a vintage Universal Geneve Polerouter automatic reference with a beautiful tropical crosshair dial from the 50s. Keeps great time and is fully functional. Measurements include a 35mm case size and 18mm lugs. On its original beads of rice bracelet that fits up to a 7 inch wrist. Dauphine hands, brown dial. Twisted lugs. Stainless steel case and caseback. The dial went from its original black color to dark brown that has different shades on the dial. The brown varies in color depending on the light. There is a scratch around 9 a clock along the dial as seen in photos. Crown is not original. All other Parts including the, dial, movement, and caseback are original. Both the dial and case are in great cosmetic, untouched condition. Ready to wear!

title: Rolex Day Date 18078 – Bark Bracelet, Lemon Diamond Dial
description: The Artisanal Side of the President The Reference 18078 is the rugged, textured sibling of the standard 18038. While the Day-Date is the universal symbol of success, the Bark finish moves the watch into a more specific tier of craftsmanship. This hand-applied decoration on the center links and bezel provides a matte, organic texture that diffuses light rather than reflecting it. It creates a "stealth-wealth" aesthetic that is immediately recognizable to collectors but less flashy than the standard high-polish execution. The "Lemon" Diamond Dial The appeal of this 1982 example is anchored by the vivid champagne dial, which leans into a bright "lemon" hue. This saturated tone provides a high-contrast backdrop for the factory diamond hour markers, creating a look that is both warm and sharp. The combination of the textured bark finish and the brilliant-cut diamonds offers a dynamic range of surfaces that makes the watch feel far more intentional than a standard production model. Remarkable Preservation: Zero Stretch Finding a 40-year-old solid gold President bracelet in this condition is a rarity. The bracelet on this example shows virtually zero stretch, retaining the original structural tension and "snap" that most gold watches of this era lose through decades of wear. The 18k yellow gold case remains thick and well-proportioned, while the day and date functions snap over crisply. After passing our test bench, the automatic movement is running strong and maintaining reliable daily time. A Significant Collector’s Spec This is the Day-Date for the person who wants the heft of solid gold but prefers the unique, hand-worked feel of a textured finish. The bark-and-lemon-diamond combination is a quintessential 1980s power play that has aged into a highly sought-after vintage icon. It is a heavy, balanced, and impeccably preserved example of Rolex’s flagship model in its most interesting architectural form.

title: 1965 Rolex Oysterdate Precision 6694 – Silver Dial
description: Here's a Rolex Oysterdate Precision reference 6694 with a silver dial manual wind from the 1960s. Measurements include a 34mm case and 19mm lug width. On its steel rivet bracelet that fits up to a 7 inch wrist. All parts including the crown, dial, movement, and caseback are original. Ready to wear!

title: 1989 Rolex Datejust 16220 - Grey Tapestry
description: A study in texture While the standard fluted bezel in white gold is the common choice, this reference features the stainless steel engine-turned bezel, and a textured grey "Tapestry" dial. The vertical ridges of the dial catch the light in a way that cannot be had with champagne dials, making it one of the most visually interesting non-stone dials Rolex produced in this era. A Cohesive All Steel Configuration This specific configuration matters because it leans into the "all-steel" look while maintaining the high-relief detail of a decorative bezel. The tapestry dial, famously associated with the 17000 series Oysterquartz but found here in a mechanical reference, adds a layer of depth that standard sunburst dials lack. It is a configuration for someone who wants a Datejust that feels different without being loud. Sharp Steel And Clean Lines The case and engine-turned bezel are in great shape, retaining their crisp edges and distinct texture. The grey tapestry dial is clean with no visible degradation or spotting. The Jubilee bracelet shows some honest stretch consistent with its age but remains fully functional and wears comfortably on the wrist. Mechanically, the watch is running strong after passing our test bench. A Distinct Daily Driver This is a watch for the person who needs one high-quality vintage piece that can be worn dailly. It has enough personality to stand out to a collector, but it’s grounded enough to never feel out of place with a t-shirt or a suit. It’s a reliable, classic 36mm Rolex that offers more texture than the average 16220.

title: 1993 Rolex Datejust 16233 - Champagne Diamond Dial
description: A Balanced Luxury Classic The Rolex Datejust 16233 is the definitive execution of the world’s most recognizable luxury watch. While the steel and gold configuration is a staple, this example is elevated by the factory champagne dial set with diamond hour markers. The 16233 transitioned the model into the modern era with the introduction of the sapphire crystal and the caliber 3135 movement, offering a level of durability and precision that remains the industry benchmark. It is a watch that carries significant visual weight without feeling dated. A Cohesive Two Tone Aesthetic The combination of 18k yellow gold and stainless steel creates a high-contrast look that defines the Datejust’s legacy. This specific configuration with the diamond-set dial pushes the watch into a more formal category while retaining the versatility of the Jubilee bracelet. It is an all-matching example that feels complete, representing the era of Rolex manufacturing where build quality and luxury finishes met in a perfectly balanced 36mm case. Strong Case And Clean Components The case and 18k gold fluted bezel are in great shape, showing sharp lines and clear definition. The champagne dial is excellent, with the factory diamond settings remaining bright and secure. The two-tone Jubilee bracelet stretch appropriate for age, but is still in good shape overall and clearly unpolished. Mechanically, the watch is running strong after passing our test bench. The Ultimate Daily Statement This is a watch for someone who wants the most iconic version of the Datejust with an added layer of prestige. It works as well with a suit as it does in a casual setting, offering a level of recognition that few other timepieces can claim. It is a reliable, classic Rolex that feels substantial and looks exactly how a luxury watch should.

title: IWC - Blue Dial
description: Here's a vintage IWC manual wind with a beautiful blue sunburst dial on a steel mesh bracelet from the 1970s. Keeps great time and is fully functional. Measurements include a 34mm case size and an integrated mesh bracelet that fits up to a 7.25 inch wrist. Snapback caseback. Manual wind caliber 403. Stainless steel case. Dotted minute track throughout the dial. A few marks on the dial as seen in photos. Blue dials from IWC in this era are not common so it's interesting to see. Makes for a great dress watch or for casual wear. Both the dial and crystal are free of any scratches or marks. All parts are original including the crown, dial, movement, and case back. Both the dial and case are in great cosmetic, untouched condition. Ready to wear!

title: Chopard St Moritz - White Dial
description: Here's a vintage Chopard Moritz in quartz with a white Roman numeral dial from the 1980s. Keeps great time and is fully functional. Recently serviced! Measurements include a 29x41mm square case size and 18mm lug width. Integrated bracelet with screws that fit up to a 7.5 inch wrist (butterfly clasp) Blue cabochon crown. White dial. Matching stick hands and hour markers. Quickset date function at 6 a clock. Nice polished finishing on both the case and bracelet. The bracelet and case shimmer, depending on the light. Drilled lug holes. 8 screws on the steel bezel. The bracelet has both a matte and polished finishing. Makes for a great watch for both men and women. A nice thin profile, makes for a great Cartier Santos alternative! All the parts are original including the crown, dial, movement, and caseback. Both the dial and case are in great cosmetic, untouched condition. Ready to wear!

title: Cartier Tank Solo - Monogram Dial
description: A Patterned Twist On Tradition The Tank Solo reference 2716 is a modern iteration of the world's most enduring watch design, but this specific example features a dial rarely seen in the standard catalog. Instead of the typical Roman numerals and railroad track, this dial is finished with a repeating "Cartier" logotype motif. This subtle textured pattern serves as a backdrop for white Arabic numerals, giving the watch a more contemporary and playful energy while maintaining the iconic rectangular silhouette. It is a configuration for the collector who values the Tank's history but wants a version that breaks from the predictable layout. A Modern Minimalist Icon The Tank Solo was designed to be a more accessible, robust entry into the Cartier family, and the 2716 in stainless steel captures that objective perfectly. At 23x30mm, it retains the classic "Louis Cartier" proportions while utilizing a flat-top case design that sits low and sharp on the wrist. Because it utilizes a high-quality quartz movement, the watch is the ultimate grab-and-go piece, requiring no winding and offering total reliability. It represents the bridge between Cartier's early 20th-century roots and the streamlined, utilitarian luxury of the 2000s. Clean Steel And Original Hardware This example is in very good condition, with the stainless steel case showing no notable scratches or dings and retaining its original factory finish. The "Motif" dial is excellent and perfectly clean, with no marks or degradation to the patterned surface. The sapphire crystal is flawless, and the watch comes equipped with its original Cartier deployant buckle on a leather strap. Mechanically, the quartz movement is running strong after passing our test bench, ensuring accurate timekeeping and a smooth ownership experience. The Versatile Daily Tank This is a watch for the person who wants a Cartier Tank that works in every possible scenario. The stainless steel construction and unique Arabic numeral dial make it feel less formal than a gold Tank Louis, yet it retains enough Cartier DNA to remain the most sophisticated object in the room. It is a durable, stylish, and increasingly hard-to-find variation of the Solo line that offers a unique visual texture without sacrificing the timeless appeal of the Tank.

title: Piaget 9151 – Blue Lapis Dial
description: An exquisite expression of 1970s haute horlogerie, this vintage Piaget reference 9151 is a rare and elegant timepiece that merges precious materials, bold design, and meticulous craftsmanship. Housed in a 25mm rectangular case crafted from solid 18k white gold, this ultra-slim, manually wound gem is paired with its original integrated white gold mesh bracelet — a hallmark of Piaget’s signature fusion of jewelry and timekeeping. The highlight is undoubtedly its deep blue lapis lazuli stone dial, punctuated with four brilliant-cut diamond markers and finished with white gold dauphine hands. Each lapis dial is unique, and this one is especially vibrant, with a rich mineral texture that subtly shifts in the light — a stunning contrast against the icy tone of the white gold. The case, bracelet, and dial are all in unpolished, untouched condition, and the original factory caseback sticker remains intact — a rare detail that speaks to the watch’s careful preservation over the decades. Mechanically, it’s fully functional and keeps excellent time, a testament to Piaget’s refined manual-wind movements of the era. All parts are 100% original, including the signed crown, dial, movement, and caseback. The integrated white gold mesh bracelet fits wrists up to 7.25 inches, draping like silk and giving the piece its unmistakably luxurious presence. This watch is more than just a rare vintage Piaget — it’s a wearable work of art, combining a lapis stone dial, diamond indices, and precious metal architecture in a way that remains utterly distinctive today. Perfect for collectors of stone-dial watches, Piaget aficionados, or anyone looking for a uniquely elegant vintage piece with real statement power. Now available exclusively through our New York City boutique or online. Don’t miss the chance to own this stunning and seldom-seen Piaget classic.

title: Hublot MDM Diver – Black Dial
description: Here's a vintage Hublot MDM Diver automatic with a black dial on a rubber strap from the 1990s. Keeps great time and is fully functional. Measurements include a 36mm steel case. It's on a Hublot rubber strap that fits up to a 7 inch wrist. Black dial with dot indices, bold luminous hands, rotating bezel, and quickset date window at 3 o'clock. Water resistant to 300 meters. Steel case and caseback. All parts are original including the signed Hublot crown, dial, movement, and caseback. Both the dial and case are in great cosmetic, untouched condition. Ready to wear!

title: Franck Muller 7501 – White Arabic Dial
description: Here's a neo-vintage Franck Muller reference 7501 automatic in 18k white gold with a white Arabic numeral dial on a leather strap from the 1990s. Keeps great time and is fully functional. Measurements include a 29x39mm case size and 16mm lug width. 18k white gold case. It's on an aftermarket pink leather strap with a signed gold buckle. Sub seconds at 6 a clock. Classic curved case design with stylized numerals and blued hands. White gold case and caseback. All parts are original including the signed Franck Muller crown, dial, movement, and caseback. Both the dial and case are in great cosmetic, untouched condition. Ready to wear!

title: Chopard St. Moritz – Two Tone White DIal
description: Refined sport-luxury, done right This vintage Chopard St. Moritz Automatic from the 1990s is a clean expression of the model at its most wearable. Executed in stainless steel and 18k yellow gold, it carries the unmistakable St. Moritz design language — integrated bracelet, sculpted case, and screw-accented bezel — in a configuration that feels both familiar and quietly elevated. Balanced, composed, and easy to live with. Why this configuration works The St. Moritz represents Chopard’s early take on refined sport-luxury, and two-tone automatic examples like this sit at the heart of the collection. The white dial keeps the watch crisp and versatile, while applied Roman numerals and a date window at six o’clock add just enough complication without cluttering the layout. The integrated steel and gold bracelet flows naturally into the case, reinforcing the sculptural design that made the St. Moritz feel ahead of its time when introduced — and still relevant today. It’s a configuration that highlights proportion and execution rather than excess, which is exactly why it wears so well decades later. Preserved as intended The watch presents in excellent, excellent condition, both cosmetically and mechanically. The case and bracelet remain well preserved, the dial is clean and evenly aged, and the overall presentation reflects careful ownership. Powered by its automatic movement, the watch is fully functional and keeps excellent time. All components are original, including the signed Chopard crown, original dial, movement, case, and caseback. A clean, confident St. Moritz This isn’t an experimental or overworked variation — it’s a strong, well-executed St. Moritz that does exactly what the model was meant to do. Elegant enough for formal settings, versatile enough for everyday wear, and distinctive without trying to stand apart. A solid vintage choice for many different reasons, and a reminder of why the St. Moritz remains one of Chopard’s most enduring designs.

title: Bvlgari Scuba SC38S – Black Dial
description: Here's a vintage Bvlgari Scuba Chronograph reference SC38S automatic with a black dial on a rubber strap from the 1990s. Keeps great time and is fully functional. Measurements include a 38mm steel case and 20mm lug width. It's on a Bvlgari signed rubber strap that fits up to a 7 inch wrist. Chronograph function with dual subdials and unidirectional rotating bezel. Matching patina in both hands and hour markers. Steel case and caseback. All parts are original including the signed Bvlgari crown, dial, movement, and caseback. Both the dial and case are in great cosmetic, untouched condition. Ready to wear!

title: Mauboussin - Yellow Gold Skeleton Dial
description: A rare fusion of horological artistry and high jewelry design, this vintage Mauboussin manual wind dress watch from the 2000s is a statement piece that reflects the bold creative spirit of one of France’s most prestigious jewelers. Crafted entirely in 18k yellow gold, it features a skeletonized black dial that reveals the inner workings of the watch through a display of visible movement components and fine finishing, all framed by a sculptural, multi-stepped bezel. The 34mm case offers strong wrist presence while maintaining an elegant, wearable profile. A clear exhibition case back gives you a second perspective on the manually wound movement, while gold dauphine hands float above the openworked mechanics, balancing modern architectural edge with classical design. The integrated 18k yellow gold bracelet features a unique finishing and a butterfly clasp, fitting wrists up to 6.75 inches. Its design recalls the classic Breguet Marine in structure and presence, lending the watch a sporty refinement while maintaining a distinctly high-luxury French aesthetic. All parts are 100% original, including the case, dial, and movement, and the watch is in great cosmetic condition, ready to be worn and admired. This piece is not just a timekeeper — it's a wearable work of modern design, offering a bold alternative to more traditional skeleton watches. Perfect for collectors of independent or jewelry-house watchmaking, or for those who want a striking, architectural gold watch with real personality and exclusivity. Now available exclusively through our New York City boutique or online. Don’t miss the chance to own this daring and elegant Mauboussin creation.

title: Baume & Mercier - Tiger Eye Dial
description: A Golden Stone Dial Classic The 1970s was an era of experimentation with organic textures, and this Baume & Mercier Tank is a definitive example of that period's aesthetic. The dial is crafted from a genuine slice of Tiger Eye stone, prized for its chatoyancy—the unique way the golden-brown bands shift and glow as they catch the light. Because it is a natural material, the striations are unique to this specific watch, offering a depth and warmth that a traditional painted dial cannot achieve. It represents a time when Baume & Mercier was at the forefront of the "stone dial" movement, merging traditional Swiss watchmaking with natural geology. A Study In Golden Warmth The 25x33mm 18k yellow gold case is the perfect frame for the Tiger Eye stone, creating a monochromatic warmth that feels substantial on the wrist. The "Tank" silhouette is a masterclass in proportion, featuring a stepped case design that adds a layer of architectural depth. Topped with a blue cabochon crown and minimalist gold dauphine hands, the watch prioritizes the raw beauty of the stone over unnecessary clutter. It is a configuration that feels intentional and artistic, sitting in that rare category of horology that functions equally well as a serious dress watch or a piece of historic jewelry. Excellent Stone And Sharp Geometry The 18k yellow gold case is in very good condition, showing only a few small nicks consistent with careful age, while maintaining its original sharp geometry. The Tiger Eye dial is the highlight of this example; it is excellent and perfectly clean, free of the hairline fractures or chips that frequently occur in vintage stone dials. The sapphire crystal is flawless, ensuring a clear view of the stone's natural chatoyancy. Mechanically, the manual-wind movement is running strong after passing our test bench, offering a tactile and reliable winding experience. The Organic Black Tie Outlier This is a watch for the collector who appreciates the "if you know, you know" prestige of a vintage stone dial. It offers a level of personality and texture that makes a standard gold dress watch feel quiet by comparison. Whether worn under a cuff for a formal event or paired with a more casual look, it remains a sharp, sophisticated statement piece. It is a rare, well-preserved artifact from an era when watch design was unapologetically bold and focused on material beauty.

title: Cartier Baignoire Allongee - Sapphire Diamond Pave
description: This ultra-rare neo-vintage Cartier Baignoire Allongée is a masterpiece of elegance, rarity, and design innovation—an extraordinary reference that transcends traditional watchmaking and flirts with high jewelry territory. Crafted in solid 18k white gold and powered by a mechanical hand-wound movement, this piece dates to the early 2000s and represents one of the most elusive and exquisite versions of Cartier’s beloved “bathtub” silhouette. The standout feature here is, without question, its factory-set sapphire dial . While most Baignoire Allongée watches feature traditional Roman numeral dials, this example showcases two tones of expertly cut natural blue sapphires arranged in a seamless, radiant mosaic. The depth, color variation, and vibrancy of the stones shift beautifully in the light, making this one of the most unique dials ever produced by Cartier. Only a handful of these sapphire-dial variants are known to exist, placing this reference among the rarest Cartier creations ever offered to the public. Its signature elongated oval case—measuring a graceful 22mm by 47mm—sits elegantly on the wrist, with its curvature allowing it to conform comfortably to nearly any wrist size. This example comes complete with an original Cartier navy blue strap and matching 18k white gold deployant clasp, a pairing that perfectly complements the deep tones of the dial while remaining classically understated. This watch has been recently serviced and is running flawlessly. It is in great and honest condition, with all parts 100% original, including the dial, case, and movement. The case retains its crisp definition, and the dial remains untouched and immaculate. This Baignoire Allongée isn’t just a rare Cartier—it’s a celebration of what makes the maison so revered: elegance, restraint, craftsmanship, and daring beauty. Whether you're a seasoned Cartier collector or someone looking to own a uniquely significant timepiece, this sapphire-dial Allongée is a trophy-level acquisition. Offered watch-only, as is common with these early 2000s pieces, and now available exclusively through our flagship New York City boutique or online. Don’t miss this once-in-a-lifetime example.

title: Vacheron Constantin - Opal Dial
description: This extraordinary vintage Vacheron Constantin is a true connoisseur’s piece—an ultra-rare creation that blends artistry, mechanical integrity, and visual spectacle into one unforgettable watch. Crafted in 18k white gold with an original integrated bracelet, the case features a highly unusual “bull’s head” design—a silhouette rarely seen in Vacheron’s vintage archive or anywhere else in mid-century watchmaking. It’s a sculptural, asymmetrical design that sets the tone for a watch that defies expectations at every turn. The real showstopper is its opal stone dial—a natural mineral known for its dynamic iridescence and shifting color patterns that dance between vibrant blues, greens, oranges, and purples depending on the light and angle. In person, the dial almost feels alive, constantly transforming with its surroundings. Genuine opal dials were produced in very limited numbers and are among the most collectible and elusive dial variations ever created—not just by Vacheron, but across all of high horology. This watch has just undergone a full professional service and is running with excellent precision. The movement, case, and dial are all 100% original and remain in remarkable condition for their age. While the caseback shows expected surface wear and scratches, it remains completely untouched and unpolished—proof of its honest history—and of course, it’s the one part you’ll never see when worn. The front, however, is a visual symphony. As a total package, this piece stands out not only for its rare opal dial and unconventional case design, but also for its preserved integrity and rarity. Vacheron Constantin has always occupied the highest tier of Swiss watchmaking, and a stone-dial model like this—especially in white gold—is the kind of once-in-a-lifetime opportunity serious collectors wait for. Whether you're drawn to rare minerals, sculptural design, or vintage Vacheron excellence, this watch checks every box. Equally at home with casual wear or black tie, it's a wearable work of art. Available now exclusively at our flagship NYC boutique or through our online platform.

title: Audemars Piguet – Two-Tone Zebra
description: Discover a truly exceptional timepiece: the Audemars Piguet “Zebra,” an ultra-rare vintage watch that stands as a testament to the brand’s bold design language and mechanical mastery. Nicknamed for its distinctive two-tone vertical stripe pattern that runs seamlessly across the case and integrated bracelet, the “Zebra” is unlike anything else on the market—a captivating blend of avant-garde aesthetics and timeless Swiss craftsmanship. This watch is not just rare; it's virtually mythical. Very few examples have ever surfaced, making it a dream acquisition for collectors who understand the value of exclusivity. Every detail of the Zebra speaks to a bygone era of daring design and meticulous engineering. The mechanical movement inside beats with the soul of traditional horology—no batteries, no shortcuts—just pure mechanical artistry. Featuring screw links for easy and precise sizing, the integrated bracelet doesn't just follow your wrist—it becomes part of it. Every stripe, every link, every turn of the crown reminds you that you’re wearing a piece of watchmaking history. This isn’t just a watch—it’s a conversation starter, a rare artifact, and a bold statement all in one. For those who value individuality, legacy, and the thrill of finding what few others ever will, the Zebra is an unrivaled treasure. Reference number- NA Crystal- Sapphire Movement- mechanical Year- 1970s Dial color- Silver & Champagne Case material- Stainless Steel Bracelet styles- Integrated bracelet fits up to 6 inch Case size- 22x30mm "Ladies" Size Bezel type- 18K Yellow Gold

title: Vacheron Constantin Extra Plat 33084  – Platinum, Silver Dial
description: Timeless Design and Mechanical Purity This rare and refined Vacheron Constantin reference 33084/000P-2 from the coveted Historiques collection exemplifies the maison’s dedication to timeless design and mechanical purity. Crafted from solid platinum, this time-only watch features a 31mm case with classic proportions and a slim, elegant profile that embodies understated luxury. Part of the Historiques line, this piece pays homage to the brand’s archival designs while offering subtle modern refinements, reinterpreting past icons with impeccable fidelity. A Study In Subtle Contrast The two-tone silver dial is a study in balance, adorned with finely printed Roman numerals and matching silver hands that offer a clean, classic aesthetic with a tactile sense of depth. The minimalist layout allows the noble metal case and dial detailing to take center stage, with every element executed to perfection. The unexpectedly hefty feel of platinum adds a richness to the wearing experience, making this piece as satisfying on the wrist as it is visually graceful. Excellent Condition And Numbered Rarity Condition: Excellent. This platinum Historiques model is a rare reference seldom seen on the market and remains in top form. The watch is fitted with its original black leather strap and Vacheron Constantin-signed platinum buckle, itself a sculptural detail that mirrors the case's elegant simplicity. The caseback is engraved with "No. 22," denoting its place in a very limited production run and further underscoring the rarity of this specific reference. As with every watch we offer, this piece has been verified as mechanically sound and is ready to wear. The Ritual Of A Manual Wind This Vacheron Constantin offers a brilliant example of restrained luxury for the collector who values heritage and rarity. The manual-wind movement creates a daily ritual and a physical bond between the owner and the machine, a core appeal of the Historiques collection. It is a superb piece for those who appreciate the unmatched feel of platinum on the wrist and a design language that remains a highly sought-after icon of haute horlogerie. Key Specs Reference - 33084/000P-2 Crystal - Sapphire Movement - Manual Wind Year - 1990’s Dial color - Silver Case material - Platinum Bracelet - Leather Strap w/ Original Buckle Case size - 31mm Bezel type - Platinum

title: Baume & Mercier – Blue Falcon Eye
description: A striking representation of 1970s luxury watchmaking, this vintage Baume & Mercier timepiece is crafted entirely in solid 18K yellow gold and features a rare and captivating blue Falcon’s Eye stone dial. Measuring 33mm across and fitted with an elegant integrated bracelet, this time-only manual wind piece showcases a perfect blend of refined case architecture and gemstone artistry. The dial is the star of the show: Falcon’s Eye, also known as Blue Tiger’s Eye, is a natural variety of quartz known for its shimmering chatoyancy and deep, silky blue hue with subtle streaks of grey and silver. While Tiger’s Eye—with its warm golden tones—is more commonly seen in vintage watch dials, Falcon’s Eye is significantly rarer, especially in factory-set examples like this. Its cooler, stormy aesthetic offers a more understated, moody elegance, making it highly sought-after among collectors of hardstone dials. Complementing the dial are sharp Dauphine hands and a matching blue cabochon-set crown, enhancing the cohesion and detail of the design. The integrated bracelet, also in 18K yellow gold, flows seamlessly from the case and gives the watch a sculptural, jewelry-like presence. The piece weighs a substantial 63.4 grams, emphasizing its quality and solid construction. Inside beats a manual wind movement, fully functional and keeping excellent time. All components—the case, dial, and movement—are 100% original and in excellent vintage condition, reflecting Baume & Mercier’s understated yet luxurious approach to watchmaking during this period. This is a truly rare and elegant example from the golden era of stone dial watches, ideal for collectors who appreciate natural materials, vintage craftsmanship, and the quiet power of distinctive design. Bracelet extending or cutting is available by request! SKU - 752

title: Cartier Tonneau - 18K Rose Gold
description: This elegant neo-vintage Cartier Tonneau Ref. 2850K in 18K rose gold captures the refined spirit of Cartier’s design language from the 1990s — a period celebrated for its timeless shapes and understated luxury. The silver guilloché dial, adorned with black Roman numerals and blued Breguet-style hands, reflects the maison’s mastery of proportion and detail. Its tonneau-shaped case wears beautifully on the wrist — slender, balanced, and unisex in its appeal. The caseback is clearly numbered with an early serial of 231, and the watch retains all the original components, including the case, dial, and manual-wind movement. This particular example is presented in mint condition, fitted with a burgundy bespoke alligator strap that perfectly complements the warmth of the rose gold case, secured by its original 18K rose gold deployant clasp. A subtle yet striking combination that enhances the watch’s timeless elegance. Pure in form and uncompromising in craftsmanship, this Tonneau embodies the essence of Cartier’s golden design era — an exquisite choice for the collector who appreciates enduring style, rarity, and refinement.

title: Chopard Onyx - Integrated Laykin & Co Stamped Double Stamped Dial
description: A Masterclass In Eighties Opulence The 1970s and 80s were a playground for Chopard, a period where they moved away from traditional watchmaking constraints to focus on high-concept jewelry pieces that happened to tell time. This Ref. 2036 1 is a heavy hitter from that era. It features a deep, bottomless onyx dial housed in a yellow gold case, but the real draw is the "double stamp" with Laykin & Co. Retailer stamps like this are historical fingerprints; Laykin & Co. was the premier luxury destination at the Fairmont Hotel in Los Angeles, catering to the Hollywood elite. Seeing their name alongside Chopard on a dial like this anchors the watch to a very specific, high-society West Coast history . Bamboo Gold And Deep Onyx The design language here is unapologetically bold. The integrated 14K yellow gold bracelet is finished in a "bamboo" style, a texture that was incredibly popular for high-end jewelry-watches during this period because of how it catches the light without looking like a standard flat link. The contrast between the organic, textured gold and the stark, sterile black of the onyx dial creates a balance that feels sophisticated rather than loud. It is a configuration that captures the exact energy of 1980s luxury . Condition And Reality The watch is in good, honest condition. The 18K yellow gold case and bezel have maintained their definition, and the integrated bracelet remains structurally sound with a total weight of nearly 100 grams, giving it a significant, high-quality presence on the wrist. The onyx dial is clean, showing no cracks or chips which is the primary concern with stone dials from this era. Mechanically, the movement is running strong after passing our test bench . Why It Works This is a piece for the collector who wants something that feels like an artifact. It sits in that perfect pocket of being a serious horological item due to the Chopard pedigree and Laykin & Co. stamp, while functioning primarily as a piece of sculptural jewelry. It works because it doesn't try to be an everyday tool; it is a specialized, era-defining statement that remains wearable today because of its refined proportions and classic color palette. It’s a distinct choice for someone who values provenance and style in equal measure .

title: Concord Year of Dragon - Enamel Dial
description: The 1-of-50 Enamel Dragon The Concord "Year of the Dragon" is a legitimate sleeper in the world of high-art horology. Produced in a strictly limited run of 50 pieces in 1998, this is an artifact of traditional craftsmanship that typically commands six-figure entries from the "Holy Trinity" houses. It features a genuine, hand-painted enamel dial—a material prized for its eternal color and depth. This isn't a mass-produced print; it is a microscopic work of art that represents a rare moment where Concord leaned into high-end artisanal execution. Hand-Painted Artistry The dial depicts a fierce, intricate dragon motif rendered in vivid enamel and accented by diamond hour markers. Enamel dials are notoriously difficult to produce, as the repeated firing process often results in cracks that ruin the entire piece. Finding a perfectly preserved example is a rarity. The 18k gold case measures 25mm x 34mm, offering a refined, rectangular silhouette that allows the artistry of the dial to be the undisputed focal point. It manages to feel both symbolic and sophisticated, providing a level of detail rarely encountered outside of bespoke commissions. Condition and Performance Verified The piece is in overall excellent vintage condition. The 18k gold case and sapphire crystal are in excellent condition, ensuring the enamel work remains protected and clearly visible. It is a healthy, verified, and exceptionally rare opportunity to own a high-level enamel dial timepiece at an accessible entry point. It represents a specific, "if you know, you know" trophy for the collector who values technical craftsmanship over brand hype. Mechanical Integrity and Provenance Unlike many decorative pieces of the era, this Limited Edition houses a manual-wind mechanical movement visible through a clear exhibition caseback. It remains fitted to its original black alligator strap and matching 18k gold buckle, preserving the original factory aesthetic. This example is especially notable for its completeness, as it is accompanied by the original papers—a critical detail for a piece with a worldwide production run of only 50 units. Key Specs Reference number - 52.55.621 Crystal - Sapphire Movement - Mechanical Year - 1998 Dial color - Enamel Case material - 18K Yellow Gold Bracelet styles - Leather Strap Case Size - 29mmx34mm Bezel type - 18K Yellow Gold SKU - 549

title: 1995 Rolex Cellini 4084 - 18K White Gold
description: Undercover Gold TV Case The Rolex Cellini 4084 is a masterclass in mid-century inspired minimalism. This reference abandons the traditional round case in favor of a softened rectangular silhouette that feels both vintage and contemporary. While Rolex is defined by its tool watches, the Cellini line exists to showcase the brand's ability to handle precious metals and delicate proportions. The silver sunburst dial is the perfect anchor for this design, catching the light in a linear fashion that complements the clean 18k white gold case. A Study In Proportional Balance At 30x28mm, this is a watch that relies on its shape rather than its size to make an impression. The integrated lug design allows the leather strap to flow directly from the case, creating a seamless look on the wrist. Because it utilizes a manual-wind movement, the profile remains incredibly thin, ensuring it sits flat and unobtrusive. It is a configuration for the collector who appreciates the technical restraint required to make a watch this simple and this effective. Excellent Case Definition And Original Hardware The 18k white gold case is in great shape, showing sharp, well-defined edges and no significant softening of its unique geometry. The silver sunburst dial is equally well-preserved, remaining clear and free of any oxidation or spotting. This example is fitted to a leather strap and retains its original Rolex 18k white gold buckle, a critical component for maintaining the watch's integrity as a complete set. Mechanically, the movement is running strong after passing our test bench and provides a smooth tactile winding experience. The Understated Choice For Formality This is a watch for the person who wants the prestige of a Rolex without the bulk of an Oyster case. It is a refined, quiet piece of horology that prioritizes texture and form over utility. Whether worn as a dedicated dress watch or a sophisticated daily alternative, it offers a level of mid-century elegance that is increasingly difficult to find in modern catalogs. It is a timeless execution of the dress watch genre.

title: Cartier Santos Galbée - Two Tone, Ivory Dial
description: The Contoured Two-Tone Classic The Santos Galbée Reference 187901 is the definitive 1990s evolution of Cartier’s most iconic silhouette. While the original 1904 design was a pioneer, the Galbée (French for "curved") update brought a necessary ergonomic flow to the square case, allowing it to hug the wrist properly. This execution in two-tone stainless steel and 18k yellow gold is the exact aesthetic that defined luxury for a generation, balancing industrial hardware with a high-jewelry finish that remains a staple of the Cartier catalog today. The Warmth of Ivory The centerpiece of this reference is the ivory Roman dial, which offers a softer, more vintage-leaning depth than the stark clinical white versions. This creamy tone provides a sophisticated bridge between the 18k yellow gold bezel and the stainless steel case. The dial layout is perfectly balanced with signature blued-steel sword hands and a railway minute track, anchored by a symmetrical quickset date window at the 6 o'clock position. It is a timeless configuration that prioritizes legibility while leaning into a richer, more classic color palette. Condition and Integrity This 187901 is an honest, untouched survivor that remains in great cosmetic shape. The ivory dial and sapphire crystal are remarkably clean, showing none of the spotting or "foxing" that can occasionally plague vintage Cartier stone or painted dials. The integrated bracelet is structurally sound and retains the classic screw-head motif, fitting up to a 6.75-inch wrist. While the high-polish gold bezel and brushed steel surfaces show some visible, honest scratches from previous wear, the case lines remain sharp and haven't been rounded off by excessive polishing. Performance Verified Mechanically, the Cartier quartz movement is performing exactly as intended, offering the "set-it-and-forget-it" convenience that makes the Galbée such a reliable daily companion. It has passed our test bench for accuracy and long-term functional health. This is a verified, ready-to-wear icon that offers a direct link to Cartier’s modern golden era. It is a healthy and imposing piece of design history that commands attention without needing to be the loudest object in the room. Key Specs Reference: 187901 Model: Santos Galbée LM (Large Model) Era: 1990s Movement: Quartz (High-Accuracy Quickset) Case Size: 29mm x 41mm Bracelet: Two-Tone Integrated (Fits up to 6.75in) Dial: Rare Grey with Blue Roman Numerals Metal: Stainless Steel & 18K Yellow Gold SKU: 186

title: Piaget Onyx & Hobnail - 18K Yellow Gold
description: This stunning Piaget Ref. 9200 from the 1980s is a standout piece from the golden era of Piaget design—bold, elegant, and unmistakably luxurious. What makes this particular example exceptionally rare is the factory onyx dial paired with a matching onyx-set bezel, creating a sleek, seamless “onyx-on-onyx” aesthetic that is virtually unheard of. Having sold multiple examples of the Ref. 9200, we can confidently say this is the first we’ve encountered in this rare configuration. The dial is a flawless slab of deep black onyx—minimalist and striking—with no hour markers to interrupt its smooth, mirror-like surface. The outer bezel is framed with the same rich onyx, perfectly intact and unchipped, providing a continuous dark-on-dark contrast that draws attention without saying a word. The inner bezel features Piaget’s signature hobnail pattern which has developed a rich oxidized look, which is mirrored across the entire bracelet, adding beautiful texture and a strong visual contrast to the sleek black dial. The integrated 18k yellow gold brick bracelet is a work of art in itself, with a fully executed hobnail finish that shimmers subtly with each movement. This bracelet remains uncut, which is extremely rare—most vintage examples have been trimmed to fit, making this untouched piece especially desirable for collectors or those looking for a full-length original bracelet. This watch is powered by a manual-wind Piaget movement, recently serviced and keeping excellent time. Known for their ultra-thin calibers, Piaget’s manual movements from this era are both technically impressive and beautifully finished. The Ref. 9200 is already an iconic representation of Piaget’s vision for luxury dress watches in the 1980s, but this onyx-on-onyx configuration takes it to another level. It offers a bold, monochrome design, elevated by the meticulous craftsmanship Piaget is known for. The uncut bracelet, perfect bezel, and clean dial make it an exceptional collector-grade example. Whether you’re looking for your first integrated gold watch or are a seasoned collector of Piaget and ultra-luxe 1980s design, this is a piece worth serious consideration. Its rarity, condition, and design synergy make it a true trophy watch—one that’s as refined as it is rare. Bracelet extensions or cutting is available by request!

title: 1969 Rolex OP 1500 - Lavender Dial
description: Lavender by design, rarely seen This Rolex Oyster Perpetual from 1969 stands out immediately for its factory lavender dial — a color Rolex produced in very limited numbers and one that rarely surfaces today. Subtle, soft, and unmistakably period-correct, it offers a completely different presence than the silver, black, or blue dials most people associate with this reference. Why this dial stands out Lavender dials occupy a quiet but important corner of vintage Rolex collecting. They weren’t loud or experimental; they were simply uncommon, produced briefly and in small quantities. The color sits somewhere between silver and purple, shifting gently depending on the light and giving the watch depth without pulling focus away from its clean Oyster Perpetual layout. Set against the smooth steel bezel and balanced case proportions, the dial adds individuality while keeping the watch grounded and wearable. It’s still very much a classic ref. 1500 — just one with a far less frequently encountered face. Condition, clearly stated Condition presents well and honestly. The case remains unpolished, showing wear consistent with age while retaining its shape. The dial is original and presents cleanly, with minor surface spotting and some visible dirt between the 8 and 9 o’clock area that should be noted. The crystal is clean. The watch is fitted to its Rolex Oyster rivet bracelet, wearing comfortably and appropriate to the era. Mechanically, the automatic movement is fully functional and keeping time as expected, with all core components remaining original. A rare-color Oyster Perpetual This isn’t a watch defined by complication or excess. It’s a classic Rolex elevated by a factory dial choice that’s genuinely difficult to replace. For someone who appreciates originality, uncommon colorways, and vintage Rolex that rewards a closer look, this ref. 1500 offers character, balance, and a quiet distinction that sets it apart from standard examples.

title: Piaget Diamond Pave - 18K White Gold

The factory pavé diamond dial adds a subtle touch of luxury, elegant without being over-the-top. For first-time buyers looking to start their collection with something truly special, this piece checks every box: timeless design, top-tier materials, and unmistakable character. It’s a statement piece, but not in a flashy way—this is for the person who appreciates quality, rarity, and detail. For collectors, this is one of those under-the-radar references that doesn’t come around often. Piaget’s ultra-thin movements and masterful design language are on full display here. The 9131 in white gold with a diamond dial is especially hard to find, making it a worthy addition to any serious collection. Dress it up or down, wear it or preserve it—either way, this watch speaks volumes. Bracelet extending or cutting is available by request!

description: A steel Datejust with a formal edge This Rolex Datejust from 1979 presents a more dress-forward take on a familiar steel reference. For someone looking for a classic steel Rolex with Roman numerals, everyday wearability, and transparent condition, this Ref. 16030 fits the bill and wears easily in rotation.

Timeless, versatile, and unmistakably Piaget, this piece transitions effortlessly from elevated everyday wear to formal occasions, exuding quiet luxury at every turn. Whether you’re a seasoned collector or discovering the world of vintage haute horology for the first time, this is a rare opportunity to own a truly unique, investment-worthy example from one of Switzerland’s most prestigious maisons. title: Rolex 19mm Yellow Gold Bracelet
description: The Final Component For A Gold Build Sourcing an original solid gold Rolex bracelet with a 19mm lug width is one of the most difficult tasks in vintage collecting. These bracelets were frequently separated from their watch heads over the decades, making a loose, period-correct example a rare find. This specific bracelet will fit a handful of different Rolex references one being the gold Daytonas and the others being 34mm oyster cases like the 1503 / 15037. This 14k gold bracelet is slightly paler and more subtle tone that defined the American market Rolex production of the 1970s and 80s. It provides a level of physical cohesion that an aftermarket strap simply cannot replicate. Factory Construction And Substantial Weight The 14K alloy used for its durability and the specific way it develops a deep, honey-colored warmth over time. This bracelet features the classic Rolex fit and finish, with tight tolerances and a clasp that operates with the mechanical precision expected from the brand. Condition And Reality The bracelet is in very good overall condition, showing the characteristic warmth and character of aged 14K gold. It has maintained its physical structure well, with the links showing consistent finishing and the clasp remaining secure. The 19mm 57 end links are intact and ready for installation on a corresponding reference. Elevating The Specification This is a necessary acquisition for anyone looking to complete a period-correct build and ensure the watch is presented exactly as it would have been when it first left the factory decades ago. This bracelet will fit on Manual-wind Daytona 6239 6241 6262 6263 6265 34mm Oyster cases (gold variants) 1008 6569 1500 1503 15037

title: 1970s Rolex Jubilee 6311 Bracelet - 14k Yellow Gold
description: Mid-Century Elegance In 14K Gold The Jubilee bracelet is a cornerstone of the Rolex aesthetic, originally introduced in 1945 to celebrate the brand's 40th anniversary. This specific reference 6311 is a rare 14k yellow gold execution, providing a warmer, more vintage-correct tone than modern 18k alloys. With its signature five-piece link construction, the Jubilee offers a level of articulation and comfort that is virtually unmatched in the world of metal bracelets. It was designed to bridge the gap between a tool-oriented Oyster bracelet and a pure dress watch, making it the ideal companion for gold mid-sized references. The Practical Utility Of 19mm End Links What distinguishes this specific 6311 is its 19mm lug width, a size that makes it compatible with specific vintage models such as the Oyster Perpetual Date reference 1503 or 15037. The bracelet maintains a classic taper toward the clasp, which is a hallmark of authentic vintage Rolex engineering. The interior of the folding clasp features the 14k hallmarking and the Registered Swiss Made stamping. This internal architecture is essential for collectors, verifying the bracelet's origin and period-correct production for the late 1960s or early 1970s. Good Condition With Authentic Character Condition: Good. This 14k yellow gold bracelet has aged honestly, showing a light patina that only comes with decades of preservation. It demonstrates a moderate amount of stretch, which is natural and expected for a gold Jubilee bracelet of this era. The links remain structurally sound with smooth articulation, and the Coronet on the clasp remains well-defined. The 19mm end pieces show some expected signs of use at the connection points but remain ready to be fitted to a compatible case. As with every item we offer, this piece has been verified as authentic and is ready to be worn. Completing The Vintage Set An original 14k gold Jubilee is the ultimate upgrade for a mid-century Rolex that has spent its life on leather. Because 19mm gold bracelets were produced in significantly lower volumes than their 20mm counterparts, finding an authentic reference 6311 in this condition is a rare opportunity for a restorer or collector. It restores the weight and visual balance that the watch was originally designed to carry. It is a piece of industrial art that transforms a vintage watch into a full-kit executive statement. title: 1996 Rolex Lady Datejust 69174 - White Motif Dial
description: Understated Elegance In White Gold And Steel The reference 69174 is the sophisticated, "undercover" alternative to the traditional two-tone Lady Datejust. By pairing a robust stainless steel case with a solid 18K white gold fluted bezel, this model offers the unmistakable sparkle of a precious metal Rolex while maintaining a monochromatic, versatile profile. It is a watch designed for the woman who appreciates the craftsmanship of a fine timepiece but prefers a more discreet aesthetic that transitions effortlessly from casual weekend wear to formal evening settings. The Intricate Silver Motif Dial The highlight of this specific 169174 is the silver "Motif" dial. Featuring a repetitive Rolex pattern across the surface, this dial adds a layer of texture and architectural depth that separates it from standard sunburst finishes. The use of polished Arabic numerals further enhances its unique identity, offering a more contemporary and readable look compared to traditional stick or Roman markers. It represents a period where Rolex began experimenting with dial textures to provide collectors with more personalized, expressive options within their core collections. Exceptional Condition And Mechanical Health This example is in very good overall condition, reflecting a well-cared-for life. The stainless steel case and Jubilee bracelet maintain their sharp lines and original factory finish, showing very few signs of wear. The 18K white gold fluted bezel remains crisp, catching the light with the brilliance that defines the Datejust line. Mechanically, the watch is an outstanding performer; it passed our test bench with flying colors. The Reliable All-Occasion Essential This is the watch for someone who wants the prestige of a Rolex without the flash of yellow gold. The combination of the textured motif dial and the white gold bezel makes it a much more interesting piece than a standard steel model, yet it remains subtle enough for any wardrobe. It works because it offers the classic 26mm "Lady" proportions in a package that feels modern and refined. It is a sharp, high-integrity, and mechanically superior example of a design that never goes out of style.
